items.en: If the weather were better, we could have a barbecue in the garden.
items.note: A modal in the main clause makes würde unnecessary.

items.hr: Ich würde sofort umziehen, wenn die Miete billiger wäre.
items.en: I would move immediately if the rent were cheaper.
items.note: Main clause first, wenn-clause after, verb still at the end.

items.hr: Hätte ich mehr Platz, würde ich einen Hund nehmen.
items.en: If I had more space, I would get a dog.
items.note: wenn can be dropped, and then the verb opens the clause.

items.hr: Wenn ich du wäre, würde ich mit dem Chef sprechen.
items.en: If I were you, I would talk to the boss.
items.note: The standard way to give advice in German.

activities.title: Irreale Bedingungen
activities.intro: Practise pairing the wenn-clause with the right main clause verb.

questions.prompt: Wenn ich mehr Zeit hätte, ___ ich öfter selbst kochen.
questions.explanation: kochen has no common simple subjunctive, so the main clause uses the subjunctive auxiliary with the infinitive at the end.

questions.prompt: Wenn das Auto ein bisschen billiger ___, würde ich es sofort kaufen.
questions.explanation: sein keeps its simple subjunctive form, and it stands at the end of the wenn-clause.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Wenn ich du ___, würde ich sofort einen Arzt anrufen.'
questions.explanation: This fixed phrase for giving advice uses the subjunctive of sein at the end of the clause.

questions.prompt: Complete with the Konjunktiv II of können: 'Wenn er besser Deutsch sprechen ___, würde er die Stelle bekommen.'
questions.explanation: The modal verb carries the mood and closes the wenn-clause after the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'If I were rich, I would travel more.'
questions.explanation: The wenn-clause fills position one, so the main clause has to begin with its verb.

activities.title: Wenn wir im Lotto gewinnen
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks what you would do with a lot of money, and you answer with unreal conditions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Was würdest du machen, wenn du im Lotto gewinnen würdest?
lines.en: What would you do if you won the lottery?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Wenn ich so viel Geld hätte, würde ich ein kleines Haus kaufen.
lines.en: If I had that much money, I would buy a small house.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und würdest du trotzdem weiter arbeiten?
lines.en: And would you still keep working?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, aber wenn ich könnte, würde ich nur noch halbtags arbeiten.
lines.en: Yes, but if I could, I would only work part time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich würde sofort kündigen, wenn ich genug Geld hätte.
lines.en: I would resign immediately if I had enough money.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Wenn ich du wäre, würde ich erst einmal in Ruhe nachdenken.
lines.en: If I were you, I would think it over calmly first.

days.title: Konjunktiv II der Vergangenheit
days.objective: Talk about regrets and missed chances with hätte or wäre plus the past participle, and reproach someone gently with hättest plus a double infinitive.
days.paretoFocus: Looking back at what should have been done differently is one of the most frequent things adults say, and German has exactly one pattern for it.
drills: Write three regrets about last week, each with hätte and a modal verb at the end.
Write two sentences with wäre plus a participle about places you did not go.
Turn two accusations into gentle reproaches beginning with Du hättest.

items: Which auxiliary do you use with verbs of movement in this past subjunctive?
Where does the modal verb stand when there is one, and in which form?
How do you say that you should have studied more?

activities.title: Das hätte ich anders gemacht
activities.intro: For a past that never happened, German uses hätte or wäre plus the past participle, choosing the same auxiliary the verb takes in the ordinary perfect. If a modal verb is involved, it appears as a plain infinitive at the very end after the other infinitive. This is the standard way to express regret and mild reproach.

items.hr: Ich hätte für die Prüfung mehr lernen sollen.
items.en: I should have studied more for the exam.
items.note: hätte plus Infinitiv plus Modalverb im Infinitiv at the end.

items.hr: Wir hätten eine Stunde früher losfahren müssen.
items.en: We should have set off an hour earlier.
items.note: Same pattern in the plural.

items.hr: Wenn ich das gewusst hätte, wäre ich zu Hause geblieben.
items.en: If I had known that, I would have stayed at home.
items.note: bleiben takes sein, so the main clause uses wäre.

items.hr: Du hättest mir das ruhig früher sagen können.
items.en: You could easily have told me that earlier.
items.note: A gentle reproach; ruhig softens it considerably.

items.hr: Ohne deine Hilfe hätte ich das nie geschafft.
items.en: Without your help I would never have managed it.
items.note: The condition can also be expressed by a simple prepositional phrase.

activities.title: Bedauern und Vorwürfe
activities.intro: Practise the past subjunctive and the choice between hätte and wäre.

questions.prompt: Wenn ich das früher gewusst ___, hätte ich ganz anders reagiert.
questions.explanation: wissen takes haben in the perfect, so the past subjunctive uses hätte, and it stands at the end of the wenn-clause.

questions.prompt: Ich ___ gestern nicht so lange arbeiten sollen.
questions.explanation: arbeiten takes haben, and the modal verb follows as a bare infinitive at the end.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Wenn du pünktlicher gewesen ___, hätten wir den Zug bekommen.'
questions.explanation: sein forms its past subjunctive with the wäre forms, and the du form takes the ending st.

questions.prompt: Complete the gentle reproach with one word: 'Du ___ mich wenigstens kurz anrufen können.'
questions.explanation: anrufen takes haben, so the du form of the subjunctive auxiliary opens the verb group.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I would have stayed at home.'
questions.explanation: bleiben belongs to the sein group, so the auxiliary is wäre and the participle stands last.

activities.title: Der verpasste Zug
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and your partner have just missed a train and talk about what you should have done.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: So ein Mist, wir haben den Zug verpasst.
lines.en: What a mess, we have missed the train.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Wir hätten wirklich eine halbe Stunde früher losfahren müssen.
lines.en: We really should have set off half an hour earlier.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Du hättest mich ja auch wecken können!
lines.en: You could have woken me up, you know!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Wenn ich gewusst hätte, dass du noch schläfst, hätte ich dich sofort geweckt.
lines.en: If I had known that you were still asleep, I would have woken you immediately.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ohne den Stau wären wir trotzdem pünktlich gewesen.
lines.en: Without the traffic jam we would have been on time anyway.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das stimmt, aber ich hätte den Weg besser planen sollen.
lines.en: That is true, but I should have planned the route better.

days.title: obwohl und trotzdem
days.objective: Express a contrast two ways, with obwohl introducing a subordinate clause and trotzdem opening a main clause, placing the verb correctly in each.
days.paretoFocus: Conceding a point and then disagreeing anyway is what turns simple statements into real opinions, and it is tested in every speaking exam.
drills: Write three pairs of sentences, saying the same idea once with obwohl and once with trotzdem.
Underline the verb in every clause you write and check its position against the rule.
Say three sentences aloud in which the obwohl-clause comes first and the main clause therefore starts with its verb.

items: Where does the verb go in a clause introduced by obwohl?
What follows immediately after trotzdem at the start of a sentence?
Rewrite one concession you made today using the other of the two words.

activities.title: Obwohl oder trotzdem
activities.intro: Both words express a contrast, but they behave completely differently. obwohl is a subordinating conjunction and sends its verb to the end of its own clause. trotzdem is an adverb that stands in first position of a main clause, so the verb comes second and the subject follows it.

items.hr: Obwohl es stark geregnet hat, sind wir spazieren gegangen.
items.en: Although it rained heavily, we went for a walk.
items.note: Verb at the end of the obwohl-clause; the main clause then begins with its verb.

items.hr: Es hat stark geregnet. Trotzdem sind wir spazieren gegangen.
items.en: It rained heavily. We went for a walk anyway.
items.note: trotzdem first, verb second, subject after the verb.

items.hr: Obwohl ich lange in der Schlange gestanden habe, habe ich keine Karten bekommen.
items.en: Although I stood in the queue for a long time, I did not get any tickets.
items.note: stehen forms the perfect with haben: ich habe gestanden.

items.hr: Mein Nachbar verdient wenig. Trotzdem kauft er sich jedes Jahr ein neues Handy.
items.en: My neighbour earns little. He still buys himself a new mobile every year.

items.hr: Obwohl sie erst seit einem Jahr hier lebt, spricht sie schon sehr gut Deutsch.
items.en: Although she has only been living here for a year, she already speaks German very well.

activities.title: Gegensätze ausdrücken
activities.intro: Practise the two contrast words and the word order that each one forces.

questions.prompt: ___ ich sehr müde war, habe ich bis Mitternacht weitergearbeitet.
questions.explanation: The verb war stands at the end of the first clause, which only a subordinating conjunction allows.

questions.prompt: Welche Wortstellung ist korrekt?
questions.explanation: trotzdem fills the first position, so the finite verb must come second and the subject follows it.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of lernen: 'Obwohl ich jeden Tag Deutsch ___, mache ich noch viele Fehler.'
questions.explanation: The conjunction pushes the finite verb to the end of its clause, where it still agrees with ich.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Das Hotel war ziemlich teuer. ___ hat es uns sehr gut gefallen.'
questions.explanation: A contrast adverb in first position is followed directly by the finite verb and then the subject.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Although the film was long, it was not boring.'
questions.explanation: The subordinate clause ends with its verb and fills position one, so the main clause opens with its own verb.

activities.title: Der erste Arbeitstag
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about your first day in a new job and you answer with concessions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wie war dein erster Arbeitstag?
lines.en: How was your first day at work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Anstrengend. Obwohl ich früh angefangen habe, bin ich erst um acht fertig geworden.
lines.en: Exhausting. Although I started early, I did not finish until eight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und gefällt dir die Stelle trotzdem?
lines.en: And do you like the job anyway?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, obwohl die Kollegen sehr direkt sind, fühle ich mich dort wohl.
lines.en: Yes, although the colleagues are very direct, I feel comfortable there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich hätte nach so einem Tag keine Lust mehr.
lines.en: After a day like that I would not feel like it any more.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich war auch müde. Trotzdem gehe ich morgen gern wieder hin.
lines.en: I was tired too. All the same I am happy to go back tomorrow.

days.title: während und seitdem
days.objective: Link two events in time with während for things happening at the same moment and seitdem for a change that has lasted since a starting point.
days.paretoFocus: These two connectors let you tell a story with layers instead of a list of separate sentences, which is the single clearest jump from beginner to independent speech.
drills: Write four sentences about your morning using während, two with a verb clause and two with a noun after während.
Describe three things that have changed in your life since a move, a new job or a new hobby, each one starting with Seitdem.
Take five short sentence pairs you already know and join each pair with während or seitdem, then say them aloud without looking.

items: Where does the conjugated verb go in a clause introduced by während?
What case does the preposition während take, and what does it sit in front of?
What tense does German normally use in a seitdem clause when the situation is still true today?

activities.title: Gleichzeitig und seit damals
activities.intro: Während introduces a subordinate clause, so the conjugated verb moves to the end. It has two jobs: two actions running at the same time, and a contrast between two people or situations. Seitdem marks the starting point of something that is still going on, and German keeps that clause in the present tense when the situation continues today. Während also works as a preposition in front of a noun, and then it takes the genitive.

items.hr: Während ich koche, deckt mein Bruder den Tisch.
items.en: While I cook, my brother sets the table.
items.note: Two actions at once; the verb koche stands at the end of the während clause.

items.hr: Während der Fahrt habe ich ein ganzes Buch gelesen.
items.en: During the journey I read a whole book.
items.note: Preposition + genitive: die Fahrt becomes während der Fahrt.

items.hr: Mein Vater ist sehr ordentlich, während meine Schwester alles liegen lässt.
items.en: My father is very tidy, whereas my sister leaves everything lying around.
items.note: Here während marks a contrast, not a shared moment.

items.hr: Seitdem ich in Hamburg wohne, fahre ich jeden Tag mit dem Rad.
items.en: Since I have been living in Hamburg, I cycle every day.
items.note: German uses the present tense because the situation is still true.

items.hr: Sie hat den Job gewechselt. Seitdem sieht man sie kaum noch.
items.en: She changed jobs. Since then you hardly ever see her.
items.note: Seitdem alone at the front of a main clause means since then; the verb comes second.

activities.title: Zwei Zeiten verbinden
activities.intro: Practise choosing between simultaneity, contrast and a starting point in the past.

questions.prompt: ___ ich dusche, hört mein Mitbewohner immer laut Radio.
questions.explanation: The two actions happen at the same moment, which is what während expresses. Seitdem would need a starting point, and nachdem would put one action before the other.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the preposition correctly with the case it requires?
options: Während des Unterrichts darf man nicht telefonieren.
Während dem Unterricht darf man nicht telefonieren.
Während der Unterricht darf man nicht telefonieren.
Während den Unterricht darf man nicht telefonieren.
questions.answer: Während des Unterrichts darf man nicht telefonieren.
questions.explanation: As a preposition während takes the genitive, so der Unterricht becomes des Unterrichts. The other forms use dative, nominative and accusative.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the connector that shows a lasting change: '___ ich weniger Kaffee trinke, schlafe ich viel besser.'
questions.explanation: The better sleep started when the coffee habit changed and it is still true, so the clause names a starting point. German keeps the present tense here.

questions.prompt: Join these into one sentence about a contrast, keeping the given order: 'Mein Bruder spart jeden Monat. Ich gebe alles sofort aus.' Write the full sentence.
questions.answer: Mein Bruder spart jeden Monat, während ich alles sofort ausgebe.
accepted: Mein Bruder spart jeden Monat, während ich alles sofort ausgebe
questions.explanation: The contrastive während sends the conjugated verb to the end, and the separable verb ausgeben comes back together as ausgebe.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'While I was cooking, my son did his homework.'
questions.explanation: The während clause comes first with habe at the end, so the main clause has to start with its verb hat.

activities.title: Was sich verändert hat
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour asks how life has been going, and you answer using während and seitdem to link your days and your changes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Man sieht dich in letzter Zeit kaum noch im Hof.
lines.en: One hardly sees you in the courtyard these days.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Seitdem ich die neue Stelle habe, komme ich erst um acht nach Hause.
lines.en: Since I have had the new job, I only get home at eight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wann kochst du dann überhaupt?
lines.en: And when do you even cook, then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Während das Wasser heiß wird, räume ich schnell die Küche auf.
lines.en: While the water is heating up, I quickly tidy the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Klingt stressig. Schläfst du wenigstens gut?
lines.en: Sounds stressful. Do you at least sleep well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Seitdem ich abends nicht mehr am Handy hänge, schlafe ich viel ruhiger.
lines.en: Since I stopped hanging on my phone in the evening, I sleep much more peacefully.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Das sollte ich auch versuchen.
lines.en: I should try that too.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meine Frau liest abends, während ich einfach früh ins Bett gehe.
lines.en: My wife reads in the evening, whereas I simply go to bed early.

days.title: bevor, nachdem und sobald
days.objective: Order two events clearly with bevor for the earlier one, nachdem for the later one and sobald for something that happens the moment the first thing is done.
days.paretoFocus: Narrating anything longer than one sentence, a working day, a trip, an accident, depends on saying reliably which event came first.
drills: Write your morning routine as four sentences, two with bevor and two with sobald.
Say aloud three things you always do the moment you get home, each starting with Sobald.
Take a nachdem sentence you wrote and check the tense: if the main clause is in the perfect, the nachdem clause must be one step further back.

items: Which of the three connectors introduces the event that happens EARLIER than the main clause?
Why does a nachdem clause almost never stand in the same tense as its main clause?
What does sobald add that nachdem does not?

activities.title: Erst das, dann das
activities.intro: All three send the conjugated verb to the end of their clause. Bevor introduces the later action's reference point, so the bevor clause names what has not happened yet. Nachdem introduces the earlier action, and this is the important one: the nachdem clause always sits one tense step further back than the main clause. Sobald works like nachdem but adds the sense of immediately, the moment that.

items.hr: Bevor ich das Haus verlasse, mache ich immer das Licht aus.
items.en: Before I leave the house, I always turn off the light.
items.note: Both clauses stay in the present; bevor already carries the order.

items.hr: Nachdem ich gefrühstückt habe, gehe ich zur Arbeit.
items.en: After I have had breakfast, I go to work.
items.note: Main clause present, nachdem clause perfect: one step back.

items.hr: Nachdem wir gegessen hatten, sind wir noch spazieren gegangen.
items.en: After we had eaten, we went for a walk.
items.note: Main clause perfect, so the nachdem clause goes back to hatten gegessen.

items.hr: Sobald der Regen aufhört, fahren wir los.
items.en: As soon as the rain stops, we set off.
items.note: Sobald means the second thing follows immediately.

items.hr: Sobald ich die Nachricht gelesen hatte, habe ich sofort zurückgerufen.
items.en: As soon as I had read the message, I called back immediately.
items.note: Sobald follows the same tense step as nachdem.

activities.title: Die richtige Reihenfolge
activities.intro: Practise putting two events in the right order and the right tense.

questions.prompt: ___ ich ins Bett gehe, putze ich mir die Zähne.
questions.explanation: Brushing the teeth happens first and going to bed second, so the clause that names the later event takes bevor.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has the tense relationship that nachdem requires?
options: Nachdem er die Prüfung bestanden hatte, hat er eine Party gefeiert.
Nachdem er die Prüfung bestanden hat, hat er eine Party gefeiert.
Nachdem er die Prüfung besteht, hat er eine Party gefeiert.
Nachdem er die Prüfung bestehen wird, hat er eine Party gefeiert.
questions.answer: Nachdem er die Prüfung bestanden hatte, hat er eine Party gefeiert.
questions.explanation: The main clause is in the perfect, so the earlier event has to move one step further back and take hatte bestanden.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap so the second action follows immediately: '___ ich zu Hause bin, rufe ich dich an.'
questions.explanation: The call comes the very moment the arrival happens, which is the sense this connector adds on top of plain sequence.

questions.prompt: Put the verb in the correct form: 'Nachdem sie lange auf dem Sofa ___ (liegen), stand sie endlich auf.'
questions.explanation: The main clause is in the Präteritum, so the earlier event needs hatte plus the participle. Standard German takes haben with liegen, so it is hatte gelegen.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Before we book the flight, I want to ask my boss.'
questions.explanation: Buchen goes to the end of the bevor clause, and because that clause fills first position, the main clause opens with möchte.

activities.title: Der Umzug am Samstag
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are planning a move with a friend and you keep making the order of the steps explicit.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wann soll ich am Samstag bei dir sein?
lines.en: When should I be at your place on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sobald du wach bist, schreib mir kurz, dann sage ich dir Bescheid.
lines.en: As soon as you are awake, drop me a line, then I will let you know.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und die schweren Kisten? Machen wir die zuerst?
lines.en: And the heavy boxes? Are we doing those first?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bevor wir die Kisten tragen, müssen wir den Schrank auseinanderbauen.
lines.en: Before we carry the boxes, we have to take the wardrobe apart.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Beim letzten Umzug war der Transporter zu klein.
lines.en: At the last move the van was too small.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nachdem wir das erlebt hatten, habe ich diesmal den großen genommen.
lines.en: After we had been through that, I took the big one this time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sehr vernünftig. Und danach essen wir was?
lines.en: Very sensible. And afterwards we eat something?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Klar, sobald die letzte Kiste oben steht, bestelle ich eine Pizza.
lines.en: Of course, as soon as the last box is upstairs, I will order a pizza.

days.title: Das Plusquamperfekt
days.objective: Form and use the Plusquamperfekt with hatte or war plus the participle to talk about what had already happened before another past event.
days.paretoFocus: It is the tense that makes a past story sound organised, and it is exactly what a nachdem clause needs, so you get two structures for the price of one.
drills: Write five Plusquamperfekt sentences about yesterday, each one naming something that had already happened before a second past event.
Take three perfect-tense sentences you know and turn them into the Plusquamperfekt by changing only the auxiliary.
Write two short stories of three sentences each, using the Präteritum for the main line and the Plusquamperfekt for the background.

items: Which two auxiliaries form the Plusquamperfekt, and in which form do they stand?
If you know the perfect of a verb, what is the only thing you have to change to get the Plusquamperfekt?
Name two verbs that take war rather than hatte in standard German.

activities.title: Was vorher schon passiert war
activities.intro: The Plusquamperfekt is simply the perfect with the auxiliary in the Präteritum: hatte or war plus the same participle you already know. It marks the event that came before another past event, so it almost never appears alone. Verbs of movement and change of state take war, everything else takes hatte, and that includes stehen, sitzen and liegen in the standard German of Germany.

items.hr: Ich hatte den Schlüssel vergessen, deshalb musste ich warten.
items.en: I had forgotten the key, so I had to wait.
items.note: Forgetting comes before waiting, so it goes one step back.

items.hr: Als wir ankamen, war der Zug schon abgefahren.
items.en: When we arrived, the train had already left.
items.note: abfahren is a movement verb, so it takes war.

items.hr: Das Auto hatte zwei Stunden im Halteverbot gestanden.
items.en: The car had been standing in the no-stopping zone for two hours.
items.note: Standard German: hatte gestanden, not war gestanden.

items.hr: Sie hatte den ganzen Nachmittag im Wartezimmer gesessen.
items.en: She had been sitting in the waiting room the whole afternoon.
items.note: sitzen also takes hatte in the standard form.

items.hr: Nachdem er die Rechnung bezahlt hatte, verließ er das Lokal.
items.en: After he had paid the bill, he left the restaurant.
items.note: The classic pair: nachdem plus Plusquamperfekt, main clause in the Präteritum.

activities.title: Einen Schritt zurück
activities.intro: Practise building the tense and choosing the right auxiliary.

questions.prompt: Als ich in die Küche kam, ___ meine Mitbewohnerin schon alles aufgeräumt.
questions.explanation: Aufräumen takes haben, and the tidying happened before the arrival, so the auxiliary stands in the Präteritum as hatte.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the auxiliary that standard German requires?
options: Der Brief hatte tagelang auf dem Tisch gelegen.
Der Brief war tagelang auf dem Tisch gelegen.
Der Brief hatte tagelang auf dem Tisch gelegt.
Der Brief war tagelang auf dem Tisch geliegen.
questions.answer: Der Brief hatte tagelang auf dem Tisch gelegen.
questions.explanation: Liegen forms its perfect with haben in the standard German of Germany, and its participle is gelegen. The war version belongs to southern usage and legen is a different verb.

questions.prompt: Put the verb into the tense that shows the earlier event: 'Er ___ (einschlafen), bevor der Film zu Ende war.'
questions.explanation: Einschlafen is a change of state, so it takes sein, and the auxiliary moves into the Präteritum to mark the earlier event.

questions.prompt: Rewrite in the tense that marks an earlier past: 'Wir haben die Wohnung besichtigt.' Write the full sentence.
questions.explanation: Only the auxiliary changes: haben moves into its Präteritum form and the participle stays exactly the same.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'After she had found the flat, she signed the contract.'
questions.explanation: The nachdem clause carries hatte gefunden at the end, and the main clause in the Präteritum starts with the verb because the subordinate clause fills first position.

activities.title: Der verpasste Termin
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You explain to a colleague why you missed an appointment, using the Plusquamperfekt for everything that had already gone wrong beforehand.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Du warst gestern gar nicht beim Termin. Was war denn los?
lines.en: You were not at the appointment yesterday at all. What happened?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Als ich losfahren wollte, war die Batterie schon leer.
lines.en: When I wanted to set off, the battery was already dead.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und der Bus? Der fährt doch alle zehn Minuten.
lines.en: And the bus? It runs every ten minutes, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nachdem ich zur Haltestelle gerannt war, war der Bus gerade abgefahren.
lines.en: After I had run to the stop, the bus had just left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hast du wenigstens angerufen?
lines.en: Did you at least call?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich hatte mein Handy zu Hause auf dem Tisch liegen lassen.
lines.en: I had left my phone at home on the table.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Was für ein Tag.
lines.en: What a day.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Am Ende hatte ich vierzig Minuten an der Haltestelle gestanden und bin wieder heimgegangen.
lines.en: In the end I had stood at the stop for forty minutes and went back home.

days.title: falls und für den Fall, dass
days.objective: Express an open, uncertain condition with falls and prepare for a possible situation with für den Fall, dass.
days.paretoFocus: Arrangements, instructions and warnings in everyday German almost always contain a condition, and falls signals clearly that you are not sure it will happen.
drills: Write four instructions for a house sitter, each one beginning with Falls.
Take three of your falls sentences and rewrite them with wenn, then note where the meaning shifts.
Write two sentences with für den Fall, dass about a trip you might take.

items: What does falls tell the listener that wenn does not?
Where does the conjugated verb stand in a falls clause?
Which part of für den Fall, dass carries the verb-final rule?

activities.title: Nur für den Notfall
activities.intro: Falls means if in the sense of in case it happens, and it signals real uncertainty. Wenn can mean the same, but it also means whenever, so falls is the clearer choice when something may simply never occur. Für den Fall, dass is the heavier version used for precautions, and the verb goes to the end of the dass part.

items.hr: Falls es regnet, nehmen wir den Bus.
items.en: If it rains, we will take the bus.
items.note: Open condition; the rain may never come.

items.hr: Falls du Fragen hast, melde dich einfach.
items.en: If you have questions, just get in touch.
items.note: Very common in emails and messages.

items.hr: Ich gebe dir meine Nummer für den Fall, dass du dich verläufst.
items.en: I will give you my number in case you get lost.
items.note: A precaution; verläufst stands at the end of the dass clause.

items.hr: Falls der Termin nicht klappt, können wir ihn verschieben.
items.en: If the appointment does not work out, we can move it.
items.note: Falls plus a modal in the main clause is very frequent.

items.hr: Nimm eine Jacke mit für den Fall, dass es abends kühl wird.
items.en: Take a jacket with you in case it gets cool in the evening.
items.note: The precaution clause can also follow the main clause.

activities.title: Wenn es doch passiert
activities.intro: Practise open conditions and precautions in everyday arrangements.

questions.prompt: ___ ich den letzten Zug verpasse, übernachte ich bei meiner Schwester.
questions.explanation: Missing the train is uncertain and may not happen at all, which is exactly the open condition this connector marks.

questions.prompt: Which sentence puts the verb in the position the structure requires?
options: Ich nehme Bargeld mit für den Fall, dass die Karte nicht funktioniert.
Ich nehme Bargeld mit für den Fall, dass funktioniert die Karte nicht.
Ich nehme Bargeld mit für den Fall, dass die Karte funktioniert nicht.
Ich nehme Bargeld mit für den Fall, die Karte nicht funktioniert.
questions.answer: Ich nehme Bargeld mit für den Fall, dass die Karte nicht funktioniert.
questions.explanation: Dass introduces a subordinate clause, so the conjugated verb has to stand at the very end, after the negation.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the connector that marks an uncertain condition: '___ du früher fertig bist, schreib mir bitte.'
questions.explanation: The speaker does not assume it will happen, so the clause needs the connector that keeps the condition open rather than habitual.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the correct present form of the separable verb meaning to arrive: 'Ich lasse den Schlüssel beim Nachbarn für den Fall, dass ich später als du ___.'
questions.explanation: In a dass clause a separable verb stays in one piece and stands last, so the prefix does not move away.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'If the shop is already closed, we will cook at home.'
questions.explanation: The auxiliary hat closes the falls clause, and since that clause occupies first position the main clause begins with kochen.

activities.title: Der Ersatzschlüssel
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are leaving for a week and giving a neighbour instructions full of open conditions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Also, ich gieße die Pflanzen. Sonst noch etwas?
lines.en: So, I will water the plants. Anything else?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Falls ein Paket kommt, nimm es bitte einfach an.
lines.en: If a parcel arrives, please just accept it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kein Problem. Und wenn irgendwas kaputtgeht?
lines.en: No problem. And if something breaks?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich schreibe dir die Nummer vom Hausmeister auf für den Fall, dass das Wasser wieder tropft.
lines.en: I will write down the caretaker's number for you in case the water drips again.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut. Wann bist du zurück?
lines.en: Good. When are you back?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Am Sonntag, aber falls der Flug gestrichen wird, erst am Montag.
lines.en: On Sunday, but if the flight gets cancelled, not until Monday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dann behalte ich den Schlüssel lieber bis Dienstag.
lines.en: Then I would rather keep the key until Tuesday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, bitte, für den Fall, dass ich es doch nicht rechtzeitig schaffe.
lines.en: Yes, please, in case I really do not make it in time.

days.title: Das Futur I und Vermutungen
days.objective: Use the present tense with a time word for ordinary future reference, and reserve werden plus infinitive for assumptions and emphatic intentions.
days.paretoFocus: Learners overuse werden and sound stiff; knowing that Germans mostly say Morgen fahre ich nach Köln, and that werden usually means I assume, fixes both directions at once.
drills: Write five plans for next week in the present tense, each with a clear time expression.
Look at three closed doors or empty desks around you and write a guess about each with werden plus wohl.
Write two promises where werden carries real emphasis, for example about paying someone back.

items: What does German normally use for a plain future statement, and what has to be present in the sentence for it to work?
Which small words often appear with werden when the sentence is a guess?
Where does the infinitive stand in a Futur I main clause?

activities.title: Zukunft und Vermutung
activities.intro: For ordinary future reference German simply uses the present tense plus a time expression, and this is what people say almost all the time. Werden plus infinitive has two real jobs: a guess about now or later, and a strong promise or intention. In guesses it usually appears with wohl, wahrscheinlich, sicher or bestimmt, and the infinitive stands at the end.

items.hr: Morgen fahre ich nach Köln.
items.en: Tomorrow I am going to Cologne.
items.note: Plain future: present tense plus morgen. This is the normal way.

items.hr: Nächstes Jahr ziehen wir in eine größere Wohnung.
items.en: Next year we are moving into a bigger flat.
items.note: Again present tense; the time word does all the work.

items.hr: Er wird wohl im Büro sein.
items.en: He is probably at the office.
items.note: A guess about right now, not about the future at all.

items.hr: Sie wird den Bus verpasst haben.
items.en: She will have missed the bus.
items.note: A guess about the past: werden plus a perfect infinitive.

items.hr: Ich werde dich nicht enttäuschen, das verspreche ich dir.
items.en: I will not let you down, I promise you that.
items.note: Here werden carries weight, a firm commitment.

activities.title: Wahrscheinlich, oder?
activities.intro: Practise separating plain future statements from assumptions.

questions.prompt: Das Licht brennt noch. Sie ___ noch arbeiten.
questions.explanation: The speaker is guessing about the present, which is the main job of werden plus infinitive. Würde would make it an irreal condition.

questions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most natural for a simple plan a German speaker would state?
options: Am Samstag treffe ich meine Eltern.
Am Samstag werde ich meine Eltern treffen werden.
Am Samstag ich werde meine Eltern treffen.
Am Samstag werde meine Eltern ich treffen.
questions.answer: Am Samstag treffe ich meine Eltern.
questions.explanation: For a straightforward plan with a time expression German uses the present tense. The other options are either doubled, or they break the rule that the conjugated verb stands in second position.

questions.prompt: Add the missing particle that turns this into a clear assumption: 'Der Chef ist noch nicht da. Er wird ___ im Stau stehen.'
questions.explanation: This little word marks the sentence as a guess rather than a prediction, and it is the most typical partner of werden in that meaning.

questions.prompt: Rewrite as a guess about the past: 'Wahrscheinlich hat er den Termin vergessen.' Begin with Er wird and write the full sentence.
questions.answer: Er wird den Termin vergessen haben.
accepted: Er wird den Termin vergessen haben
questions.explanation: A guess about a past event uses werden plus the participle plus haben, and both infinitive parts stand at the end.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I promise you I will pay the money back.'
questions.explanation: In a dass clause the conjugated verb stands last of all, so the infinitive zurückzahlen comes before werde.

activities.title: Wo bleibt Jonas?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend has not turned up and you keep guessing what has happened, using werden for assumptions and the present tense for the actual plan.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jonas ist immer noch nicht da. Hast du was von ihm gehört?
lines.en: Jonas still is not here. Have you heard anything from him?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nein, aber er wird wieder im Stau stehen, das ist bei ihm normal.
lines.en: No, but he is probably stuck in traffic again, that is normal for him.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oder er hat den Termin verwechselt.
lines.en: Or he mixed up the time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Möglich. Er wird die Nachricht wohl nicht gelesen haben.
lines.en: Possible. He probably will not have read the message.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sollen wir schon bestellen?
lines.en: Should we order already?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, ich bestelle jetzt, und um acht gehe ich sowieso nach Hause.
lines.en: Yes, I am ordering now, and at eight I am going home anyway.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dann sag ihm bitte noch kurz Bescheid.
lines.en: Then please let him know quickly.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mache ich. Ich werde ihn ganz sicher nicht einfach sitzen lassen.
lines.en: I will. I definitely will not just leave him hanging.

days.title: Das Verb lassen
days.objective: Use lassen in its three everyday meanings: to leave something somewhere, to allow someone to do something, and above all to have something done by someone else.
days.paretoFocus: The third meaning has no simple English equivalent and covers half of adult life, from haircuts to car repairs, so without lassen you cannot say what you had done.
drills: Write four sentences about services you pay for, each with lassen in the have it done meaning.
Write three sentences where lassen means to leave an object somewhere, and say where each thing is.
Turn two of your sentences into the perfect and check that you use the double infinitive form.

items: What are the three meanings of lassen, with one example for each?
In Ich lasse mein Auto reparieren, who is doing the repair?
How does lassen behave in the perfect tense when it stands with another verb?

activities.title: Drei Verben in einem
activities.intro: Lassen is really three verbs. On its own it means to leave something somewhere. With another verb in the infinitive it means either to allow someone to do something or, most often, to arrange for someone else to do it for you. The vowel changes in the singular, du lässt and er lässt, and in the perfect with a second verb lassen appears as an infinitive, not as gelassen.

items.hr: Ich habe meinen Regenschirm im Büro gelassen.
items.en: I left my umbrella at the office.
items.note: Lassen alone: to leave an object somewhere. Participle gelassen.

items.hr: Meine Eltern lassen mich am Wochenende länger schlafen.
items.en: My parents let me sleep in longer at the weekend.
items.note: Meaning to allow; the infinitive schlafen stands at the end.

items.hr: Ich lasse mein Auto in der Werkstatt reparieren.
items.en: I am having my car repaired at the garage.
items.note: The key meaning: someone else does the work for me.

items.hr: Sie lässt sich die Haare schneiden.
items.en: She is having her hair cut.
items.note: With sich when the service is done to your own body.

items.hr: Wir haben die Heizung prüfen lassen.
items.en: We had the heating checked.
items.note: Perfect with a second verb: two infinitives, prüfen lassen, never gelassen.

activities.title: Machen lassen statt selbst machen
activities.intro: Practise the three meanings and the forms that go with them.

questions.prompt: Ich kann nicht kochen, also ___ ich mir jeden Abend etwas liefern.
questions.explanation: The subject is ich, so the form is lasse, and the meaning is that somebody else does the delivering.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is the correct perfect of Wir lassen die Fenster putzen?
options: Wir haben die Fenster putzen lassen.
Wir haben die Fenster putzen gelassen.
Wir sind die Fenster putzen lassen.
Wir haben die Fenster geputzt gelassen.
questions.answer: Wir haben die Fenster putzen lassen.
questions.explanation: When lassen stands with another verb, its perfect uses the double infinitive, so the participle gelassen does not appear.

questions.prompt: Put the verb in the correct present form: 'Warum ___ (lassen) du deine Schuhe immer im Flur stehen?'
questions.explanation: The vowel changes in the second person singular, and the spelling takes ss because the vowel is short.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence about a service: 'Mein Anzug ist schmutzig, ich ___ ihn morgen reinigen.'
questions.explanation: The speaker is not cleaning the suit personally, so the sentence needs the verb that hands the job to someone else, in the ich form.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I am having my glasses repaired on Friday.'
questions.explanation: The conjugated lasse stands in second position and the infinitive reparieren closes the sentence, with the time expression before the object.

activities.title: Alles in der Werkstatt
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are telling a colleague about a busy week of appointments, and almost everything is being done for you by someone else.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Du siehst müde aus. Viel zu tun diese Woche?
lines.en: You look tired. A lot to do this week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ziemlich. Morgen lasse ich endlich die Bremsen kontrollieren.
lines.en: Quite a bit. Tomorrow I am finally having the brakes checked.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Machst du so etwas nie selbst?
lines.en: Do you never do that kind of thing yourself?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nein, davon verstehe ich nichts, das lasse ich lieber die Werkstatt machen.
lines.en: No, I know nothing about it, I would rather have the garage do it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und der Termin am Donnerstag?
lines.en: And the appointment on Thursday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da lasse ich mir die Haare schneiden, das war längst nötig.
lines.en: That is when I am getting my hair cut, it was long overdue.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hast du eigentlich die Wohnung schon streichen lassen?
lines.en: Have you actually had the flat painted yet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, im Mai, aber die Farbdosen habe ich im Keller gelassen.
lines.en: Yes, in May, but I left the paint tins in the cellar.

days.title: Partizipien als Adjektive
days.objective: Turn present and past participles into adjectives, das schlafende Kind and das reparierte Auto, with the normal adjective endings you already use.
days.paretoFocus: It compresses a whole relative clause into one word, which is how written and spoken German stays short without losing information.
drills: Take four relative clauses you have written before and compress each into a participle adjective.
Describe a room around you with five noun phrases, each containing a participle as an adjective.
Sort ten participles you know into ones that describe an ongoing action and ones that describe a finished result.

items: How do you build a present participle from an infinitive?
Which participle describes something still happening, and which one describes a finished result?
Do participle adjectives take special endings, or the same ones as any other adjective?

activities.title: Das schlafende Kind
activities.intro: The present participle is the infinitive plus d, so schlafen becomes schlafend, and it describes an action still going on. The past participle you already know, repariert or gekocht, describes a finished result. Both stand in front of a noun like any adjective and take exactly the endings you have already learnt, so nothing new has to be memorised there.

items.hr: Bitte weck das schlafende Kind nicht.
items.en: Please do not wake the sleeping child.
items.note: schlafen plus d gives schlafend, then the normal ending.

items.hr: Das reparierte Auto steht wieder vor der Tür.
items.en: The repaired car is in front of the door again.
items.note: Past participle: the work is finished.

items.hr: Wir haben eine überraschende Nachricht bekommen.
items.en: We received a surprising piece of news.
items.note: Present participle after an indefinite article takes the ending e here.

items.hr: Zum Frühstück gibt es frisch gekochte Eier.
items.en: For breakfast there are freshly boiled eggs.
items.note: The result is done; an adverb can stand in front of the participle.

items.hr: In der wartenden Schlange standen fast dreißig Leute.
items.en: In the waiting queue there were almost thirty people.
items.note: Dative after in, so the participle takes the ending en.

activities.title: Kurz statt Nebensatz
activities.intro: Practise building participles and giving them the right ending.

questions.prompt: Der Hund bellt. Der ___ Hund stört die Nachbarn.
questions.explanation: The barking is still going on, so it is the present participle bellend, and after der in the nominative the adjective ending is e.

questions.prompt: Which noun phrase describes a result that is already finished?
questions.explanation: The past participle marks a completed action, so the washing has been done. The other forms describe something ongoing or still to be done.

questions.prompt: Build the adjective from the verb in brackets: 'Wir mussten wegen der ___ (steigen) Preise sparen.'
questions.explanation: The rise is ongoing, so the infinitive takes d, and after wegen with the genitive plural the adjective ends in en.

questions.prompt: Compress this relative clause into one adjective: 'Das Fenster, das jemand geöffnet hat, steht immer noch offen.' Write only the adjective that would stand before Fenster.
questions.explanation: A finished action becomes the past participle, and in the neuter nominative after das the adjective takes the ending e.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'The waiting passengers were getting impatient.'
questions.explanation: The plural after die takes the ending en, and the conjugated verb wurden stands in second position.

activities.title: Im Fundbüro
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are describing a lost bag, and every description uses a participle as an adjective.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, was haben Sie denn verloren?
lines.en: Hello, what have you lost?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Eine dunkelblaue Tasche mit einem kaputten Reißverschluss.
lines.en: A dark blue bag with a broken zip.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: War etwas Wichtiges darin?
lines.en: Was there anything important inside?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, ein unterschriebener Vertrag und ein gerade gekauftes Ladegerät.
lines.en: Yes, a signed contract and a charger I had just bought.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wir haben hier eine passende Tasche, aber sie ist ziemlich schmutzig.
lines.en: We have a matching bag here, but it is rather dirty.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das könnte meine sein. Steht da ein aufgeklebter Name drauf?
lines.en: That could be mine. Is there a stuck-on name on it?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja, hier auf der Innenseite.
lines.en: Yes, here on the inside.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dann bin ich der erleichterte Besitzer, vielen Dank.
lines.en: Then I am the relieved owner, thank you very much.

days.title: Nomen-Verb-Verbindungen
days.objective: Use fixed noun and verb pairs such as eine Entscheidung treffen, Rücksicht nehmen and zur Verfügung stehen as single memorised units.
days.paretoFocus: These combinations are what makes German sound adult rather than translated, and they are fixed, so the wrong verb is immediately noticeable.
drills: Write five sentences at work or at home using a different fixed pair in each.
Cover the verbs in your list and try to produce them from the nouns alone, then check.
Rewrite three simple sentences you already know, replacing a plain verb with a fixed pair.

items: Which verb goes with eine Entscheidung, and which one with eine Frage?
What does Rücksicht nehmen mean, and which preposition follows it?
Name two of these pairs that use a preposition plus a noun instead of an article plus a noun.

activities.title: Feste Verbindungen
activities.intro: In these expressions a noun and a verb belong together permanently, and you cannot swap the verb for a synonym. English often uses a single verb where German uses a pair, so translating word by word goes wrong. Learn each one as a whole block, together with any preposition it brings along.

items.hr: Wir müssen bis Freitag eine Entscheidung treffen.
items.en: We have to make a decision by Friday.
items.note: treffen, never machen, with die Entscheidung.

items.hr: Darf ich Ihnen eine Frage stellen?
items.en: May I ask you a question?
items.note: stellen with die Frage; fragen alone takes no noun here.

items.hr: Bitte nehmen Sie Rücksicht auf die anderen Gäste.
items.en: Please show consideration for the other guests.
items.note: die Rücksicht plus nehmen, followed by auf with the accusative.

items.hr: Für Rückfragen stehe ich Ihnen gern zur Verfügung.
items.en: I am glad to be available to you for questions.
items.note: Fixed with zu plus die Verfügung; very common in emails.

items.hr: Nach dem Gespräch sind wir mit der Firma in Kontakt getreten.
items.en: After the conversation we got in touch with the company.
items.note: der Kontakt plus treten, and treten takes sein in the perfect.

activities.title: Welches Verb passt?
activities.intro: Practise the fixed verb that belongs to each noun.

questions.prompt: Der Vorstand muss heute eine wichtige Entscheidung ___.
questions.explanation: This noun is locked to treffen. Machen is a direct translation from English and sounds wrong to a German ear.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses a fixed pair correctly?
options: Unsere Kollegin nimmt immer Rücksicht auf jüngere Mitarbeiter.
Unsere Kollegin macht immer Rücksicht auf jüngere Mitarbeiter.
Unsere Kollegin gibt immer Rücksicht auf jüngere Mitarbeiter.
Unsere Kollegin stellt immer Rücksicht auf jüngere Mitarbeiter.
questions.answer: Unsere Kollegin nimmt immer Rücksicht auf jüngere Mitarbeiter.
questions.explanation: The noun combines only with nehmen, and the expression continues with auf plus the accusative.

questions.prompt: Supply the verb that belongs to this noun: 'Der Lehrer hat mir eine schwierige Frage ___.'
questions.explanation: This noun always pairs with stellen, and in the perfect it appears as the participle.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite formula in an email: 'Bei weiteren Fragen stehen wir Ihnen jederzeit zur ___.'
questions.explanation: The noun in this fixed expression follows zur and means being available to somebody. It is capitalised like every German noun.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Yesterday we got in touch with the landlord.'
questions.explanation: The block in Kontakt treten stays together at the end, and treten forms its perfect with sein.

activities.title: Das Teamgespräch
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are in a short work meeting where every turn uses a fixed noun and verb pair.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wir sollten das Thema heute abschließen.
lines.en: We should finish this topic today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Einverstanden, aber vorher möchte ich eine Frage stellen.
lines.en: Agreed, but first I would like to ask a question.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bitte, nur zu.
lines.en: Please, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Wer trifft am Ende die Entscheidung über das Budget?
lines.en: Who makes the decision about the budget in the end?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Die Abteilungsleitung, aber unsere Meinung zählt.
lines.en: The department management, but our opinion counts.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut. Dann trete ich morgen mit der Buchhaltung in Kontakt.
lines.en: Good. Then I will get in touch with the accounting department tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Brauchst du dafür noch Zahlen von mir?
lines.en: Do you need any figures from me for that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nur den Jahresbericht. Ich stehe für Rückfragen zur Verfügung.
lines.en: Just the annual report. I am available for follow-up questions.

days.title: Wortbildung, Vorsilben und Nachsilben
days.objective: Decode unfamiliar German words by recognising the prefixes un-, ver-, be-, ent- and er- and the endings -bar, -los, -ung, -heit and -keit.
days.paretoFocus: German builds new words out of old ones, so a handful of building blocks turns hundreds of unknown words into guessable ones without a dictionary.
drills: Take five adjectives you know and test whether un- gives a real opposite, then check the ones that feel odd.
Turn six verbs you know into nouns with -ung and write the article in front of each.
Find four long words in anything you read today and split each one into its parts before guessing the meaning.

items: What do the endings -bar and -los add to a word stem?
Which article do nouns ending in -ung, -heit and -keit always take?
What sense does ver- often carry, and what does be- typically do to a verb's object?

activities.title: Bausteine der Wörter
activities.intro: Un- negates an adjective, -bar says that something can be done, and -los says that something is missing. The endings -ung, -heit and -keit build nouns out of verbs and adjectives, and all three are feminine, so the article is always die. The inseparable prefixes carry rough senses: ver- often means wrong or away, be- makes a verb take a direct object, ent- means removing or escaping, and er- often means achieving or bringing about.

items.hr: Der Text ist unklar, aber die Handschrift ist noch lesbar.
items.en: The text is unclear, but the handwriting is still legible.
items.note: un- negates klar; -bar says lesen is possible.

items.hr: Nach dem Streit war er tagelang sprachlos.
items.en: After the argument he was speechless for days.
items.note: -los marks absence: die Sprache is missing.

items.hr: Die Wohnung ist nach der Renovierung viel heller.
items.en: The flat is much brighter after the renovation.
items.note: renovieren gives die Renovierung; every -ung noun is feminine.

items.hr: Die Gesundheit und die Pünktlichkeit sind ihm sehr wichtig.
items.en: Health and punctuality are very important to him.
items.note: -heit and -keit turn adjectives into feminine nouns.

items.hr: Ich habe den Zug verpasst und musste die Fahrkarte entwerten lassen.
items.en: I missed the train and had to have the ticket validated.
items.note: ver- as going wrong; ent- as taking something away.

activities.title: Wörter zerlegen
activities.intro: Practise reading unknown words by their parts and building new ones yourself.

questions.prompt: Ein Antrag, den man online schicken kann, ist online ___.
questions.explanation: The ending -bar states that an action is possible, which is exactly what can be sent means. The other endings mark absence, an abstract noun or a negation.

questions.prompt: Which word means the state of being free, built from an adjective?
questions.explanation: Abstract nouns from adjectives take -heit or -keit and are always feminine, so the article is die.

questions.prompt: Build one word meaning without work from die Arbeit: 'Seit der Kündigung ist mein Nachbar ___.'
questions.explanation: The ending that marks absence attaches to the stem, and as an adjective in the predicate it takes no ending.

questions.prompt: Turn the verb into a noun and add its article: 'Wir warten noch auf ___ des Vertrags.' (verlängern)
questions.explanation: The ending -ung builds a noun from the verb, every such noun is feminine, and it is capitalised.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Unfortunately the machine is no longer repairable.'
questions.explanation: The sentence opens with leider, so the verb ist takes second position, and the -bar adjective stands last as a predicate with no ending.

activities.title: Ein schwieriger Brief
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend does not understand an official letter, and you help by breaking the long words into parts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich verstehe diesen Brief überhaupt nicht. Was heißt Bearbeitungszeit?
lines.en: I do not understand this letter at all. What does Bearbeitungszeit mean?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das kommt von bearbeiten, also die Zeit, die sie für die Bearbeitung brauchen.
lines.en: That comes from bearbeiten, so the time they need to process it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und hier steht unvollständig. Ist das schlimm?
lines.en: And here it says unvollständig. Is that bad?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un- macht das Gegenteil, also nicht vollständig, es fehlt noch etwas.
lines.en: Un- makes the opposite, so not complete, something is still missing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ganz unten steht kostenlos. Das klingt gut.
lines.en: Right at the bottom it says kostenlos. That sounds good.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Genau, -los heißt ohne, in diesem Fall ohne Kosten.
lines.en: Exactly, -los means without, in this case without costs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und die Verlängerung, die sie erwähnen?
lines.en: And the extension they mention?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Von verlängern. Alle Wörter auf -ung sind feminin, deshalb die Verlängerung.
lines.en: From verlängern. All words ending in -ung are feminine, which is why it is die Verlängerung.

days.title: Indirekte Rede im Alltag
days.objective: Report what someone else said the way people really do in spoken German, with dass or a plain statement in the indicative, adjusting pronouns and time words.
days.paretoFocus: Passing on messages is one of the most frequent things anyone does in a second language, at work, at home and on the phone.
drills: Report four things a friend told you this week, two with dass and two without.
Take three direct quotations and rewrite them, changing every pronoun and time word that needs to shift.
Report two questions, one with ob and one with a question word.

items: What are the two everyday ways to report a statement, and how does the word order differ?
Which word introduces a reported yes or no question?
What has to change besides the verb when you report someone else's words?

activities.title: Weitersagen, was jemand gesagt hat
activities.intro: In everyday spoken German you report speech with the indicative, not with any special verb form. You either use dass and send the verb to the end, or you simply leave dass out and keep normal main clause order. What really has to change are the pronouns and the time and place words, because you are speaking from your own position now. There is a special form used in news writing, but nobody uses it in conversation.

items.hr: Er hat gesagt, dass er heute später kommt.
items.en: He said that he is coming later today.
items.note: With dass the verb kommt stands at the end.

items.hr: Sie meint, sie hat im Moment keine Zeit.
items.en: She says she has no time at the moment.
items.note: Without dass the clause keeps normal word order.

items.hr: Meine Kollegin hat erzählt, dass sie umgezogen ist.
items.en: My colleague said that she has moved house.
items.note: Pronoun shift: ich in her words becomes sie in yours.

items.hr: Er hat gefragt, ob wir am Wochenende Zeit haben.
items.en: He asked whether we have time at the weekend.
items.note: Reported yes or no question uses ob.

items.hr: Sie wollte wissen, wann der Kurs anfängt.
items.en: She wanted to know when the course starts.
items.note: With a question word, that word introduces the clause.

activities.title: Eine Nachricht weitergeben
activities.intro: Practise passing on statements and questions with the right connector and the right pronouns.

questions.prompt: Tom fragt: 'Kommst du mit?' Tom hat gefragt, ___ ich mitkomme.
questions.explanation: A reported yes or no question needs ob. Dass is for statements and wenn is a condition.

questions.prompt: Anna sagt: 'Ich habe meinen Schlüssel verloren.' Which report is correct?
options: Anna hat gesagt, dass sie ihren Schlüssel verloren hat.
Anna hat gesagt, dass ich meinen Schlüssel verloren hat.
Anna hat gesagt, dass sie ihren Schlüssel hat verloren.
Anna hat gesagt, dass sie hat ihren Schlüssel verloren.
questions.answer: Anna hat gesagt, dass sie ihren Schlüssel verloren hat.
questions.explanation: The pronouns move from her position to yours, and in a dass clause the conjugated hat stands at the very end.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the right pronoun. Anna: 'Ich brauche meinen Ausweis.' Anna hat gesagt, dass ___ ihren Ausweis braucht.
questions.explanation: You are speaking from your own position, so the first person of the original words becomes the third person in your report.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the connector that turns the reported part into a subordinate clause: 'Mein Chef sagt, ___ der Termin morgen ausfällt.'
questions.explanation: This connector is the usual way to report a statement, and it pushes the conjugated verb to the end, which is why the separable verb stays in one piece there.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'She told me that she is looking for a new flat.'
questions.explanation: The reporting clause comes first, and inside the dass clause the conjugated verb sucht stands last.

activities.title: Was der Vermieter gesagt hat
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your flatmate was out, so you pass on everything the landlord said and asked.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: War der Vermieter wirklich da? Was wollte er?
lines.en: Was the landlord really here? What did he want?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Er hat gesagt, dass die Handwerker nächste Woche kommen.
lines.en: He said that the workmen are coming next week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wegen der Heizung?
lines.en: And about the heating?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Er meint, das Problem ist schon bekannt und wird bald behoben.
lines.en: He says the problem is already known and will be fixed soon.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hat er sonst noch etwas wissen wollen?
lines.en: Did he want to know anything else?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, er hat gefragt, ob wir am Donnerstag zu Hause sind.
lines.en: Yes, he asked whether we are at home on Thursday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da arbeite ich bis sechs. Wollte er eine Uhrzeit?
lines.en: I am working until six that day. Did he want a time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Er wollte wissen, wann du normalerweise zurückkommst, ich sage ihm morgen Bescheid.
lines.en: He wanted to know when you normally get back, I will let him know tomorrow.

days.title: Umwelt und Klimawandel
days.objective: State a clear position on climate change in German and back it up with a reason, using the passive and Konjunktiv II.
days.paretoFocus: Climate is the single most common opinion topic in B1 speaking and writing tasks, and the sentence frames used here work for any controversial subject.
drills: Write three sentences that begin 'Ich bin davon überzeugt, dass ...' and add a reason with 'weil' or 'denn'.
Turn five active sentences about pollution into the passive with 'wird' or 'werden'.
Say aloud what you would change about your own energy use, using 'Wenn ich ... würde, ...'.

items: How do you build the present passive with 'durch' to name the cause?
Which relative pronoun follows a neuter noun in the nominative?
Give two ways to introduce a reason after stating an opinion.

activities.title: Eine Meinung zum Klima begründen
activities.intro: At this level an opinion on its own is not enough: the exam always wants the reason attached to it. Combine an opinion frame with 'weil' or 'denn', and use the passive when the actor does not matter. Konjunktiv II lets you talk about what would happen under different conditions.

items.hr: Der Klimawandel ist die Herausforderung, die unsere Generation am meisten beschäftigt.
items.en: Climate change is the challenge that occupies our generation the most.
items.note: Relativsatz im Nominativ: die Herausforderung, die ...

items.hr: Ich bin davon überzeugt, dass wir schnell handeln müssen, denn die Folgen sind schon heute sichtbar.
items.en: I am convinced that we have to act quickly, because the consequences are already visible today.
items.note: Position plus Grund: überzeugt sein von etwas, hier mit 'davon, dass'.

items.hr: In den Städten wird die Luft durch den Verkehr stark belastet.
items.en: In the cities the air is heavily polluted by traffic.
items.note: Passiv Präsens; die Ursache steht mit 'durch' plus Akkusativ.

items.hr: Wenn jeder ein bisschen weniger verbrauchen würde, wäre schon viel gewonnen.
items.en: If everyone consumed a little less, a lot would already be gained.
items.note: Konjunktiv II: würde plus Infinitiv im Nebensatz, wäre im Hauptsatz.

items.hr: Ich halte strenge Verbote für den falschen Weg, weil Menschen ihr Verhalten nur freiwillig dauerhaft ändern.
items.en: I consider strict bans the wrong way, because people only change their behaviour permanently when they do it voluntarily.
items.note: etwas für etwas halten: eine feste Wendung für eine Bewertung.

activities.title: Passiv, Relativsatz und Konjunktiv II
activities.intro: Practise expressing a position with the structures that make it sound like a real argument.

questions.prompt: Ergänzen Sie: In vielen Ländern ___ der Ausstoß von Treibhausgasen genau gemessen.
questions.explanation: The subject is 'der Ausstoß', which is singular, so the present passive takes 'wird' plus the participle 'gemessen'.

questions.prompt: Welcher Satz drückt eine Bedingung im Konjunktiv II korrekt aus?
options: Wenn wir weniger fliegen würden, wäre der Ausstoß niedriger.
Wenn wir weniger fliegen werden, ist der Ausstoß niedriger.
Wenn wir weniger fliegen würden, ist der Ausstoß niedriger gewesen.
Wenn wir weniger fliegen, wären der Ausstoß niedriger.
questions.answer: Wenn wir weniger fliegen würden, wäre der Ausstoß niedriger.
questions.explanation: An unreal condition needs Konjunktiv II in both halves: 'würden' plus infinitive in the wenn-clause and 'wäre' in the main clause.

questions.prompt: Ergänzen Sie das Relativpronomen: Das ist ein Thema, ___ uns alle betrifft.
questions.explanation: 'Thema' is neuter and the pronoun is the subject of the relative clause, so it is 'das' in the nominative.

questions.prompt: Ergänzen Sie die Konjunktion, die einen Nebensatz mit einem Gegensatz einleitet: ___ die Folgen längst bekannt sind, ändert sich wenig.
questions.explanation: 'Obwohl' introduces a concessive subordinate clause and sends the conjugated verb to the end, here 'sind'.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I am convinced that we have to act quickly.'
questions.explanation: The da-compound 'davon' points forward to the dass-clause, in which the modal verb stands last.

activities.title: Streit über den Klimaschutz
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your neighbour thinks the whole debate is exaggerated, so defend your position with reasons rather than just repeating it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich finde diese ganze Klimadebatte übertrieben. Das Wetter war doch schon immer wechselhaft.
lines.en: I find this whole climate debate exaggerated. The weather has always been changeable.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Wechselhaft ja, aber die Durchschnittstemperatur steigt seit Jahrzehnten, und das wird weltweit gemessen.
lines.en: Changeable yes, but the average temperature has been rising for decades, and that is measured worldwide.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Selbst wenn das stimmt, kann ein einzelnes Land kaum etwas ändern.
lines.en: Even if that is true, a single country can hardly change anything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das sehe ich anders. Wenn alle so denken würden, würde sich nie etwas bewegen.
lines.en: I see that differently. If everyone thought like that, nothing would ever move.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Trotzdem darf man die Kosten nicht vergessen. Für viele Familien wird das einfach zu teuer.
lines.en: Nevertheless you must not forget the costs. For many families it simply becomes too expensive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da hast du recht, das ist ein echtes Problem. Deshalb bin ich dafür, dass die Kosten gerechter verteilt werden.
lines.en: There you are right, that is a real problem. That is why I am in favour of the costs being distributed more fairly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Damit könnte ich leben, solange niemand überfordert wird.
lines.en: I could live with that, as long as nobody is overwhelmed.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Genau darüber sollten wir streiten, nicht darüber, ob es den Klimawandel überhaupt gibt.
lines.en: That is exactly what we should argue about, not about whether climate change exists at all.

days.title: Nachhaltig leben im Alltag
days.objective: Talk about everyday sustainable habits using zu-infinitives, verbs with fixed prepositions and the da-compounds.
days.paretoFocus: The zu-infinitive frames 'es lohnt sich', 'ich achte darauf' and 'es bringt wenig' let you comment on almost any habit or plan.
drills: Write four sentences with 'Es lohnt sich, ... zu ...' about things you do at home.
List five verbs with a fixed preposition and build a da-compound for each.
Describe one habit you gave up and say honestly whether it was worth it.

items: Where does 'zu' go in a separable verb inside a zu-infinitive?
Which preposition follows 'achten' and which da-compound belongs to it?
Name two Nomen-Verb-Verbindungen and the verb each one needs.

activities.title: Kleine Gewohnheiten, große Wirkung
activities.intro: German loves fixed frames that are completed by a zu-infinitive. Several very common verbs also require a fixed preposition, and when the object is a whole clause the preposition becomes a da-compound. Noun plus verb combinations such as 'eine Entscheidung treffen' sound far more natural than a single simple verb.

items.hr: Es lohnt sich, die Geräte ganz auszuschalten, statt sie im Bereitschaftsbetrieb zu lassen.
items.en: It is worth switching the devices off completely instead of leaving them on standby.
items.note: Trennbares Verb im zu-Infinitiv: aus-zu-schalten.

items.hr: Ich achte darauf, nur das zu kaufen, was ich wirklich brauche.
items.en: I make sure to buy only what I really need.
items.note: achten auf plus Akkusativ, vor einem Nebensatz: darauf.

items.hr: Viele Menschen treffen die Entscheidung, weniger zu besitzen, und fühlen sich danach freier.
items.en: Many people make the decision to own less and feel freer afterwards.
items.note: Nomen-Verb-Verbindung: eine Entscheidung treffen, nicht machen.

items.hr: Ich finde Mehrweg besser als Einweg, weil eine Flasche viele Male benutzt werden kann.
items.en: I find reusable better than single use, because one bottle can be used many times.
items.note: Passiv mit Modalverb: benutzt werden kann.

items.hr: Meiner Meinung nach bringt es wenig, einzelne Produkte zu meiden, denn entscheidend ist der gesamte Verbrauch.
items.en: In my opinion it achieves little to avoid individual products, because what matters is total consumption.
items.note: Position plus Grund mit 'denn', danach normale Wortstellung.

activities.title: Zu-Infinitiv und feste Präpositionen
activities.intro: Practise the frames that turn a habit into a full B1 sentence.

questions.prompt: Ergänzen Sie: Ich interessiere mich ___, wie viel Strom mein Haushalt verbraucht.
questions.explanation: 'sich interessieren' takes 'für', so before a subordinate clause the da-compound is 'dafür'.

questions.prompt: Welche Form des trennbaren Verbs ist richtig: Es ist sinnvoll, das Licht ___.
questions.explanation: In a separable verb the 'zu' is placed between the prefix and the stem and everything is written as one word.

questions.prompt: Ergänzen Sie die fehlende Präposition: Ich achte sehr ___ meinen Wasserverbrauch.
questions.explanation: 'achten' has the fixed preposition 'auf' with the accusative, here 'meinen Wasserverbrauch'.

questions.prompt: Ergänzen Sie das passende Verb: Gestern habe ich endlich eine Entscheidung ___.
questions.explanation: The fixed combination is 'eine Entscheidung treffen', and the participle of 'treffen' is 'getroffen'.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'It is worth repairing old devices.'
questions.explanation: 'Es lohnt sich' is followed by a zu-infinitive group in which 'zu' stands directly before the infinitive at the end.

activities.title: Bringt das überhaupt etwas?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend doubts that individual habits matter at all, so weigh the two sides instead of insisting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Du kaufst unverpackt ein und reparierst alles selbst. Ehrlich gesagt halte ich das für reine Symbolik.
lines.en: You shop package free and repair everything yourself. Honestly, I consider that pure symbolism.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Symbolisch ist es teilweise schon, aber es lohnt sich trotzdem, weil ich dabei deutlich weniger Geld ausgebe.
lines.en: It is partly symbolic, but it is still worth it, because I spend noticeably less money that way.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Das ist ein Argument. Nur achtest du auf jede Kleinigkeit, während die großen Entscheidungen ganz woanders getroffen werden.
lines.en: That is an argument. Only you pay attention to every little thing, while the big decisions are made somewhere else entirely.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da gebe ich dir recht. Ich glaube aber, dass Gewohnheiten die Meinung verändern, und daraus entsteht später Druck.
lines.en: There I agree with you. But I believe that habits change opinions, and pressure grows out of that later.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vielleicht. Mich stört eher, dass viele damit anderen ein schlechtes Gewissen machen wollen.
lines.en: Maybe. What bothers me more is that many people want to give others a bad conscience with it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das finde ich auch falsch. Ich erzähle nur davon, wenn jemand fragt.
lines.en: I think that is wrong too. I only talk about it when someone asks.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: So kann ich damit leben. Zeig mir doch mal, wie du das mit den Geräten machst.
lines.en: In that case I can live with it. Show me sometime how you do it with the devices.

days.title: Mülltrennung und Recycling, genauer betrachtet
days.objective: Explain the German deposit system, what happens to sorted waste and why a contaminated bin is a problem, using the passive and the genitive.
days.paretoFocus: Deposit machines, bin rules and shared-house arguments about them are unavoidable in daily life in Germany, and the passive is the natural voice for describing processes.
drills: Describe in five passive sentences what happens to a bottle from purchase to return.
Sort ten items you have at home into the correct bin and say why in German.
Explain in two sentences why a wrongly filled bin can ruin a whole load.

items: What is a Pfand and how do you get it back?
How do you form the past passive with 'wurde'?
Which genitive article follows a neuter noun such as 'das Recycling'?

activities.title: Pfand, Tonnen und saubere Trennung
activities.intro: Processes are usually described in the passive, because the actor is unimportant. You already know the bins from the earlier lesson, so this time the focus is on the deposit system, on what happens after collection and on why one dirty item can spoil a whole container. The genitive appears naturally with abstract nouns such as 'die Qualität des Recyclings'.

items.hr: Auf viele Flaschen und Dosen wird ein Pfand erhoben, das man am Automaten zurückbekommt.
items.en: A deposit is charged on many bottles and cans, which you get back at the machine.
items.note: Passiv Präsens plus Relativsatz im Akkusativ: das Pfand, das ...

items.hr: Altglas wird nach Farben getrennt gesammelt, weil gemischtes Glas schlechter wiederverwertet werden kann.
items.en: Waste glass is collected separately by colour, because mixed glass can be recycled less well.
items.note: Passiv mit Modalverb im Nebensatz: wiederverwertet werden kann.

items.hr: Wenn eine Tonne falsch befüllt wird, kann die ganze Ladung am Ende verbrannt werden.
items.en: If a bin is filled incorrectly, the whole load can end up being burned.
items.note: Verunreinigung: ein falscher Gegenstand macht die Sortierung wertlos.

items.hr: Ich halte das Pfandsystem für sinnvoll, weil es einen echten Anreiz schafft, die Flasche zurückzubringen.
items.en: I consider the deposit system sensible, because it creates a real incentive to bring the bottle back.
items.note: Position plus Grund, danach ein zu-Infinitiv mit trennbarem Verb.

items.hr: Die Qualität des Recyclings hängt vor allem davon ab, wie sauber zu Hause getrennt wird.
items.en: The quality of recycling depends above all on how cleanly waste is separated at home.
items.note: Genitiv des Recyclings; abhängen von plus da-Verbindung davon.

activities.title: Prozesse im Passiv beschreiben
activities.intro: Practise describing what happens to waste and where each item belongs.

questions.prompt: Wohin gehört eine saubere, trockene Zeitung?
questions.explanation: Clean paper belongs in the paper collection; only paper that is greasy or wet goes into the residual waste.

questions.prompt: Ergänzen Sie im Passiv Präteritum: Früher ___ viel weniger Müll getrennt.
questions.explanation: The past passive uses the Präteritum of 'werden' plus the participle, so 'wurde ... getrennt'.

questions.prompt: Ergänzen Sie das fehlende Nomen: Leere Flaschen bringe ich zum Automaten und bekomme dort das ___ zurück.
questions.explanation: 'das Pfand' is the deposit you paid when buying the drink; the machine pays it back on return.

questions.prompt: Ergänzen Sie den Artikel im Genitiv: die Qualität ___ Recyclings
questions.explanation: Neuter nouns take 'des' in the genitive and the noun itself adds an ending, here 'Recyclings'.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Glass is collected separately by colour.'
questions.explanation: In the present passive the participle stands at the end, so 'gesammelt' closes the sentence after 'getrennt'.

activities.title: Diskussion an der Mülltonne
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your flatmate thinks careful sorting is pointless, so argue with concrete reasons and concede the fair points.
