lines.en: They do have time, but nothing is done with it. I am interested in characters who make decisions, not in characters who only talk.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dann hättest du bis zur sechsten Folge bleiben müssen. Ab da ändert sich alles.
lines.en: Then you should have stayed until the sixth episode. Everything changes from there on.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Darauf will ich mich nicht verlassen. Eine Serie, die erst nach sechs Stunden anfängt, hat ein Problem.
lines.en: I do not want to rely on that. A series that only gets going after six hours has a problem.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da bin ich anderer Meinung, aber gut. Worauf hattest du denn zuletzt richtig Lust?
lines.en: I disagree, but fine. So what did you really feel like watching most recently?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Auf eine kurze Serie mit acht Folgen. Damit war ich an einem Wochenende fertig und trotzdem zufrieden.
lines.en: A short series with eight episodes. I finished it in one weekend and was still satisfied.

days.title: Musik und Konzerte
days.objective: Talk about music and live concerts and give reasons, alternatives and omissions with the whole zu-infinitive family.
days.paretoFocus: um zu, ohne zu and statt zu let you pack purpose, contrast and alternatives into one clean sentence, which is exactly what makes spoken German sound adult rather than choppy.
drills: Say three reasons why you do something musical, each with um zu.
Say two things you did without doing something else, using ohne zu.
Say two alternatives with statt zu, for example a small venue instead of a big one.

items: Where does zu go in a separable verb inside an infinitive clause?
What condition must be met before you can use um zu?
How do you say that something is worth doing?

activities.title: Über Musik und Konzerte sprechen
activities.intro: The zu-infinitive is one structure with several jobs: um zu gives a purpose, ohne zu says what did not happen alongside, and statt zu gives the alternative that was rejected. All three require the same subject in both parts of the sentence. With separable verbs the zu slides between the prefix and the stem.

items.hr: Ich gehe ins Konzert, um die Musik einmal live zu erleben, nicht um Aufnahmen zu vergleichen.
items.en: I go to concerts in order to experience the music live for once, not in order to compare recordings.
items.note: um zu gives the purpose. Both parts share the subject ich.

items.hr: Sie ist gegangen, ohne sich die Zugabe anzuhören.
items.en: She left without listening to the encore.
items.note: ohne zu. With the separable verb anhören the zu goes inside: anzuhören.

items.hr: Statt teure Karten zu kaufen, gehen wir lieber zu einem kleinen Auftritt im Viertel.
items.en: Instead of buying expensive tickets, we prefer to go to a small performance in the neighbourhood.
items.note: statt zu names the rejected alternative and can stand in front of the main clause.

items.hr: Es lohnt sich, früh da zu sein, weil die besten Plätze schnell weg sind.
items.en: It is worth being there early, because the best spots go quickly.
items.note: Es lohnt sich plus zu-infinitive. The es is a placeholder for the whole infinitive clause.

items.hr: Ich habe vor, im Sommer auf ein Festival zu fahren, obwohl ich Menschenmengen nicht mag.
items.en: I intend to go to a festival in the summer, although I do not like crowds.
items.note: vorhaben plus zu-infinitive. After obwohl the conjugated verb mag goes to the end.

activities.title: Infinitivsätze mit zu
activities.intro: Practise choosing between um, ohne and statt and placing zu correctly.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct link: 'Er hat den Saal verlassen, ___ sich zu verabschieden.'
questions.explanation: The sentence says that the goodbye did not happen, which is exactly what ohne zu expresses. damit is impossible here because it never combines with a zu-infinitive.

questions.prompt: Which sentence places zu correctly with a separable verb?
options: Ich habe versucht, das Lied mitzusingen.
Ich habe versucht, das Lied zu mitsingen.
Ich habe versucht, das Lied mit zu singen.
Ich habe versucht, das Lied mitsingen zu.
questions.answer: Ich habe versucht, das Lied mitzusingen.
questions.explanation: With separable verbs the zu is inserted between the prefix and the stem and everything is written as one word: mitzusingen.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conjunction of purpose: 'Wir sind früher losgefahren, ___ pünktlich anzukommen.'
questions.explanation: A purpose with the same subject in both parts is expressed by um plus zu-infinitive. The zu already sits inside anzukommen.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing particle: 'Ich habe keine Lust, heute Abend noch ___ tanzen.'
questions.explanation: Lust haben is followed by an infinitive clause, and every such infinitive carries the particle in front of the plain verb.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Instead of buying expensive tickets, we listen to the concert on the radio.'
questions.explanation: The statt-clause stands in position one, so the main clause begins with the conjugated verb hören and the subject follows it.

activities.title: Konzert oder Aufnahme?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner thinks live concerts are a waste of money and you argue for them, using zu-infinitives.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Achtzig Euro für ein Konzert? Dafür kaufe ich mir lieber Musik und höre sie zu Hause.
lines.en: Eighty euros for a concert? I would rather buy music with that and listen to it at home.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das kann ich nicht nachvollziehen. Man geht doch nicht hin, um perfekten Klang zu bekommen, sondern um dabei zu sein.
lines.en: I cannot follow that. You do not go there in order to get perfect sound, you go in order to be there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dabei sein heißt bei mir: zwei Stunden warten, ohne etwas zu sehen.
lines.en: For me, being there means waiting for two hours without seeing anything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dann suchst du dir die falschen Säle aus. In kleinen Clubs steht man dicht vor der Bühne, statt in einer riesigen Halle zu verschwinden.
lines.en: Then you are choosing the wrong venues. In small clubs you stand right in front of the stage instead of disappearing in a huge hall.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Trotzdem bleibt es teuer, und die Aufnahme klingt zu Hause einfach besser.
lines.en: It stays expensive all the same, and the recording simply sounds better at home.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sauberer, ja. Besser finde ich sie nicht, weil die Stimmung im Raum fehlt.
lines.en: Cleaner, yes. I do not find it better, because the atmosphere in the room is missing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Also gut, ich komme einmal mit. Aber du suchst den Abend aus.
lines.en: All right, I will come along once. But you choose the evening.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Abgemacht. Es lohnt sich, so etwas wenigstens einmal auszuprobieren, bevor man es ablehnt.
lines.en: Agreed. It is worth trying something like that at least once before you reject it.

days.title: Bücher und Lesen
days.objective: Talk about books and reading habits, tell what happened in what order with the Plusquamperfekt, and describe things with participles used as adjectives.
days.paretoFocus: Participles as adjectives compress a whole relative clause into one word, and nachdem plus Plusquamperfekt is how German keeps a sequence of events clear.
drills: Tell the story of the last thing you read in four sentences, using nachdem at least once.
Turn three relative clauses into participles used as adjectives, for example a text that was translated carefully.
Say two opinions about reading on paper versus reading on a screen and give a reason for each.

items: How do you build the Plusquamperfekt, and which tense follows nachdem?
What is the difference in meaning between the first and the second participle used as an adjective?
How do you say that a novel is well written?

activities.title: Über Bücher sprechen
activities.intro: The Plusquamperfekt is the past before the past, built with hatte or war plus the Partizip II, and after nachdem it is almost obligatory. German also loves to turn participles into adjectives: the Partizip II carries a passive meaning, the Partizip I an active one, and both take normal adjective endings.

items.hr: Nachdem ich die ersten fünfzig Seiten gelesen hatte, wusste ich, dass mich das Buch nicht mehr loslässt.
items.en: After I had read the first fifty pages, I knew that the book would not let go of me.
items.note: nachdem takes the Plusquamperfekt, and the main clause moves one step forward in time.

items.hr: Ein gut geschriebener Roman braucht keine überraschende Wendung am Ende.
items.en: A well written novel does not need a surprising twist at the end.
items.note: Partizip II as adjective: geschrieben plus the ending er. It has a passive meaning, something that was written.

items.hr: Eine ständig wachsende Zahl von Leuten liest wieder auf Papier.
items.en: A constantly growing number of people are reading on paper again.
items.note: Partizip I: infinitive plus d plus ending. It has an active meaning, something that is growing.

items.hr: Das Buch, über das gerade alle sprechen, hat mich enttäuscht, obwohl es sorgfältig geschrieben ist.
items.en: The book that everyone is talking about right now disappointed me, although it is carefully written.
items.note: sprechen über plus accusative gives the relative clause 'über das'. The preposition stands in front of the pronoun.

items.hr: Ich lese abends zwanzig Minuten, nicht um schneller zu werden, sondern um zur Ruhe zu kommen.
items.en: I read for twenty minutes in the evening, not in order to get faster, but in order to calm down.
items.note: Fixed phrase: zur Ruhe kommen. Two purposes are contrasted with nicht um zu, sondern um zu.

activities.title: Partizipien und Vorzeitigkeit
activities.intro: Practise the past before the past and participles used as adjectives.

questions.prompt: Choose the conjunction: '___ ich das Buch zurückgegeben hatte, fiel mir eine wichtige Stelle wieder ein.'
questions.explanation: The Plusquamperfekt hatte zurückgegeben shows that this event came first, and the conjunction for that order is nachdem. Bevor would need the reverse sequence.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct participle used as an adjective: 'ein sorgfältig ___ Text'
questions.explanation: The text was translated, so the meaning is passive and you need the Partizip II with a masculine nominative ending after ein, which gives übersetzter.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing auxiliary: 'Als der Roman erschien, ___ die Autorin ihn schon zweimal umgeschrieben.'
questions.explanation: Rewriting happened before the publication, so the earlier event stands in the Plusquamperfekt, formed with the past form of haben plus the Partizip II.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition in the relative clause: 'Das Buch, ___ das alle sprechen, ist schnell ausverkauft.'
questions.explanation: sprechen is fixed with über plus the accusative, and in a relative clause the preposition moves in front of the relative pronoun.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'After I had finished the novel, I immediately lent it to a friend.'
questions.explanation: The nachdem-clause takes the Plusquamperfekt with hatte at the end, and the main clause then follows with the conjugated verb in first position.

activities.title: Buch oder Verfilmung?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner has stopped reading and prefers screen versions of stories, and you push back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich lese kaum noch. Wenn eine Geschichte gut ist, sehe ich sie mir lieber als Film an.
lines.en: I hardly read any more. If a story is good, I would rather watch it as a film.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Damit verlierst du aber das Wichtigste. Ein Film zeigt dir alles, ein Buch lässt dich selbst denken.
lines.en: But that way you lose the most important thing. A film shows you everything, a book lets you think for yourself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Das klingt schön, kostet aber zwölf Stunden statt zwei.
lines.en: That sounds nice, but it costs twelve hours instead of two.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Und genau diese Stunden verändern etwas. Nachdem ich einen langen Roman gelesen hatte, hat mich die Geschichte wochenlang begleitet.
lines.en: And it is exactly those hours that change something. After I had read a long novel, the story stayed with me for weeks.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mir passiert das auch nach guten Filmen. Der Unterschied ist kleiner, als du denkst.
lines.en: That happens to me after good films too. The difference is smaller than you think.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vielleicht. Trotzdem bleibt eine sorgfältig geschriebene Szene länger im Kopf, weil man sie sich selbst vorstellen muss.
lines.en: Perhaps. All the same, a carefully written scene stays in your head longer, because you have to imagine it yourself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut, dann leih mir etwas Kurzes. Bei fünfhundert Seiten steige ich sofort aus.
lines.en: Fine, then lend me something short. With five hundred pages I give up immediately.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Abgemacht. Ich habe genau das Richtige, und danach reden wir noch einmal darüber.
lines.en: Agreed. I have exactly the right thing, and afterwards we will talk about it again.

days.title: Sport und Vereinsleben
days.objective: Talk about sport and about joining a Verein, and handle the vocabulary of membership, fees and training.
days.paretoFocus: The Verein is one of the most reliable ways to meet people in a German-speaking country, and newcomers underuse it simply because they do not know how it works.
drills: Say aloud what you would need to ask before joining a club: fee, training times, trial session.
Practise the perfect of sitzen, stehen and liegen in five sentences about a training evening.
Explain in three sentences why a club is a good way to meet people.

items: Which case does beitreten take, and what is the noun for the fee a member pays?
How do you say that training takes place twice a week?
Which auxiliary do sitzen, stehen and liegen take in the standard perfect?

activities.title: Im Verein aktiv werden
activities.intro: A Verein is a registered club, and German social life runs on them: sport, music, gardening, volunteer fire brigades and much more. Joining one means paying a small regular fee and turning up, and in return you get a fixed group of people who see you every week. The vocabulary is small and pays for itself immediately.

items.hr: Ein Verein ist ein Zusammenschluss von Menschen, die ein gemeinsames Interesse haben, vom Sport bis zur Musik.
items.en: A Verein is an association of people who share a common interest, from sport to music.
items.note: Relative clause with die in the nominative plural, referring to Menschen.

items.hr: Wer einem Verein beitreten will, zahlt meistens einen monatlichen Beitrag und wird dann Mitglied.
items.en: Anyone who wants to join a club usually pays a monthly fee and then becomes a member.
items.note: beitreten takes the dative: einem Verein. Der Beitrag is the fee, das Mitglied the member.

items.hr: Das Training findet zweimal in der Woche statt, und danach sitzen viele noch lange zusammen.
items.en: Training takes place twice a week, and afterwards many people sit together for a long time.
items.note: stattfinden is separable: findet statt. Das Training and der Trainer are everyday club words.

items.hr: Gestern hat die ganze Mannschaft nach dem Spiel noch eine Stunde im Vereinsheim zusammengesessen.
items.en: Yesterday the whole team sat together in the clubhouse for another hour after the match.
items.note: Standard German: hat gesessen and hat zusammengesessen, never with sein.

items.hr: Einem Verein beizutreten ist eine der einfachsten Möglichkeiten, in einer neuen Stadt Leute kennenzulernen.
items.en: Joining a club is one of the easiest ways to meet people in a new town.
items.note: A zu-infinitive can be the subject. Separable verbs put zu inside: beizutreten, kennenzulernen.

activities.title: Verein, Mitgliedschaft und Training
activities.intro: Practise club vocabulary, the dative after beitreten and the standard perfect forms.

questions.prompt: Choose the standard auxiliary: 'Nach dem Training ___ wir noch eine halbe Stunde im Vereinsheim gesessen.'
questions.explanation: In the standard German of Germany sitzen forms the perfect with haben. The version with sind is a southern regional variant, and the standard form is haben gesessen.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct case after beitreten: 'Ich bin ___ Sportverein beigetreten.'
questions.explanation: beitreten governs the dative, so a masculine noun takes einem. The verb itself forms its perfect with sein.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for the money a member pays every month: 'Der monatliche ___ liegt bei fünfzehn Euro.'
questions.explanation: Der Beitrag is the standard word for a membership fee, and like every German noun it is capitalised.

questions.prompt: Complete the separable verb: 'Das Training ___ jeden Dienstag um achtzehn Uhr statt.'
questions.explanation: The verb is stattfinden. In a main clause the stem is conjugated in second position and the prefix goes to the very end.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Joining a club is the easiest way to meet people.'
questions.explanation: The zu-infinitive acts as the subject and stands in position one. Both separable verbs place zu between prefix and stem.

activities.title: Soll ich in einen Verein eintreten?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner is sceptical about clubs and you argue that joining one is the fastest way to meet people.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ein Verein? Das klingt nach Sitzungen, Beiträgen und Leuten, die seit dreißig Jahren dasselbe machen.
lines.en: A club? That sounds like meetings, fees and people who have been doing the same thing for thirty years.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: So habe ich früher auch gedacht, aber das stimmt einfach nicht. Beim Training redet niemand über Formulare.
lines.en: I used to think that too, but it is simply not true. At training nobody talks about forms.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich kann doch allein laufen gehen, ohne jeden Monat zu zahlen.
lines.en: But I can go running on my own without paying every month.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Laufen kannst du allein, aber Leute lernst du dabei nicht kennen. Genau dafür ist ein Verein da.
lines.en: You can run alone, but you will not meet anyone that way. That is exactly what a club is for.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wenn mir die Gruppe nicht gefällt, hänge ich ein Jahr lang in der Mitgliedschaft fest.
lines.en: And if I do not like the group, I am stuck in the membership for a whole year.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das ist ein berechtigter Einwand. Trotzdem kann man fast überall zweimal zum Probetraining kommen, bevor man Mitglied wird.
lines.en: That is a fair objection. All the same, almost everywhere you can come to a trial session twice before you become a member.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zwei Termine, ohne gleich zu unterschreiben? Damit könnte ich anfangen.
lines.en: Two sessions without signing anything straight away? I could start with that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Genau. Und falls es passt, hast du nach drei Monaten mehr Kontakte als nach drei Jahren allein im Park.
lines.en: Exactly. And if it suits you, after three months you will have more contacts than after three years alone in the park.

days.title: Wissenschaft und Forschung im Gespräch
days.objective: Talk about a study or a discovery in everyday language, express how certain you are, and ask someone to explain something more simply.
days.paretoFocus: Saying how sure you are, and asking politely for a simpler explanation, works in every conversation you will ever have, not only in a scientific one.
drills: Say the same claim four times, each time with a different degree of certainty.
Turn three active sentences about a study into the passive.
Practise two polite requests for a simpler or slower explanation.

items: Which participle does the passive perfect use instead of geworden?
How do you say that something could be the case without claiming that it is?
How do you politely ask someone to explain something again in simpler words?

activities.title: Über Forschung sprechen
activities.intro: Talking about research in German needs surprisingly little technical vocabulary. What you do need is the passive, because studies are reported without naming who did what, and a scale of certainty so that you do not overstate a result. The polite Konjunktiv II then lets you ask for a simpler version of anything you did not follow.

items.hr: In einer neuen Studie wurde untersucht, wie sich zu wenig Schlaf auf die Konzentration auswirkt.
items.en: A new study investigated how too little sleep affects concentration.
items.note: Passive in the past: wurde untersucht. sich auswirken auf plus accusative.

items.hr: Sicher ist das noch nicht, aber wahrscheinlich hängen die beiden Dinge zusammen.
items.en: That is not certain yet, but the two things are probably connected.
items.note: A scale of certainty: sicher, wahrscheinlich, vermutlich, vielleicht, in that order.

items.hr: Es könnte sein, dass die Untersuchung zu wenige Teilnehmer hatte.
items.en: It could be that the study had too few participants.
items.note: Es könnte sein, dass expresses a cautious possibility with the Konjunktiv II of können.

items.hr: Könnten Sie mir das bitte noch einmal einfacher erklären? Den zweiten Teil habe ich nicht ganz verstanden.
items.en: Could you please explain that to me again in simpler terms? I did not quite understand the second part.
items.note: Polite request with könnten. Naming the part you missed makes the answer far more useful.

items.hr: Nachdem die Ergebnisse veröffentlicht worden waren, wurde die Studie in vielen Zeitungen diskutiert.
items.en: After the results had been published, the study was discussed in many newspapers.
items.note: Passive Plusquamperfekt: worden waren. In the passive it is worden, never geworden.

activities.title: Passiv und Sicherheitsgrade
activities.intro: Practise passive forms and the words that show how sure you are.

questions.prompt: Choose the present passive: 'Die Daten ___ von mehreren Gruppen unabhängig geprüft.'
questions.explanation: The present passive is werden plus the Partizip II. sind geprüft would describe a finished state, not the ongoing process.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses the weakest degree of certainty?
questions.explanation: The Konjunktiv II könnte only opens a possibility, while wahrscheinlich already claims a likelihood and sicher and bestimmt state near certainty.

questions.prompt: Complete the passive perfect: 'Die Studie ist im Frühjahr veröffentlicht ___.'
questions.explanation: In the passive perfect the participle of werden loses its ge, so it is worden and not geworden.

questions.prompt: Complete with the polite modal verb: '___ Sie mir das bitte etwas einfacher erklären?'
questions.explanation: The Konjunktiv II of können makes a request polite. The present form Können Sie is possible but sounds noticeably more direct.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'It could be that the study had too few participants.'
questions.explanation: Es könnte sein is followed by a dass-clause, and in that clause the conjugated verb hatte stands at the very end.

activities.title: Was sagt die Studie wirklich?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner repeats a headline about a study and you argue for caution, using the passive and careful degrees of certainty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hast du gehört? Es wurde festgestellt, dass Kaffee das Gedächtnis verbessert.
lines.en: Have you heard? It has been established that coffee improves memory.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: So einfach ist das bestimmt nicht. Weißt du, wie viele Personen überhaupt untersucht wurden?
lines.en: It is certainly not that simple. Do you know how many people were actually examined?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Keine Ahnung, aber es stand überall. Dann wird schon etwas dran sein.
lines.en: No idea, but it was everywhere. So there must be something to it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das geht mir zu schnell. Es könnte auch sein, dass nur ein Zusammenhang gefunden wurde und keine Ursache.
lines.en: That is too quick for me. It could also be that only a connection was found and no cause.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jetzt machst du es unnötig kompliziert. Für mich reicht: Kaffee hilft.
lines.en: Now you are making it unnecessarily complicated. For me it is enough that coffee helps.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Könntest du mir kurz zuhören? Ein Zusammenhang heißt nur, dass zwei Dinge zusammen auftreten. Warum das so ist, ist damit noch nicht geklärt.
lines.en: Could you listen to me for a moment? A connection only means that two things occur together. Why that is so has not been settled by it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut, das leuchtet mir ein. Trotzdem trinke ich morgen früh meinen Kaffee.
lines.en: All right, that makes sense to me. All the same, I will drink my coffee tomorrow morning.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dagegen sage ich gar nichts. Ich sage nur: wahrscheinlich, nicht sicher.
lines.en: I have nothing against that at all. I am only saying probably, not certainly.

days.title: Lesen: die Hauptaussage schnell finden
days.objective: You can identify the main point of a German text within a minute by reading the questions first and scanning for the answer instead of reading every word.
days.paretoFocus: The reading module gives you 65 minutes for several texts, so the candidates who pass are the ones who hunt for the main idea rather than translating each sentence.
drills: Take any German article, read only the headline plus the first and last sentence, then write the main point in one English sentence.
Set a timer for 60 seconds and underline the single sentence in a paragraph that carries the whole argument.
Cover a German text, read three questions about it first, then read the text once and answer without looking back.

items: How many modules does the exam have, and what happens if you fail only one of them?
What do you read first, the text or the questions, and why does the order matter?
Name two German expressions that signal a writer's central point.

activities.title: Zuerst die Fragen, dann der Text
activities.intro: The exam is modular: four separate parts, each passed or failed on its own at sixty percent, and only a failed part has to be retaken. That means a strong reading result cannot rescue a weak listening result, so plan your effort part by part. In reading, the fastest gain comes from reading the questions before the text.

items.hr: Die Prüfung besteht aus vier Modulen, und jedes Modul wird einzeln bewertet.
items.en: The exam consists of four modules, and each module is assessed separately.
items.note: das Modul, die Module

items.hr: Man besteht ein Modul mit sechzig Prozent, und nur ein nicht bestandenes Modul muss man wiederholen.
items.en: You pass a module with sixty percent, and you only have to repeat a module you have failed.
items.note: bestehen, hat bestanden

items.hr: Lies zuerst die Fragen, dann weißt du beim Lesen genau, wonach du suchst.
items.en: Read the questions first, then while reading you know exactly what you are looking for.
items.note: wonach = nach was

items.hr: Der wichtigste Satz steht oft am Anfang oder am Ende eines Abschnitts.
items.en: The most important sentence is often at the beginning or at the end of a paragraph.
items.note: der Abschnitt, die Abschnitte

items.hr: Ausdrücke wie 'vor allem', 'insgesamt' und 'entscheidend ist' zeigen die Hauptaussage an.
items.en: Expressions like 'vor allem', 'insgesamt' and 'entscheidend ist' point to the main message.
items.note: anzeigen, zeigt an, hat angezeigt

activities.title: Die Hauptaussage erkennen
activities.intro: Practise spotting the sentence that carries the message and recalling how the modular result works.

questions.prompt: Which sentence most clearly signals the writer's main point?
options: Entscheidend ist, dass viele Jugendliche zu wenig schlafen.
Am Montag hat der Verein ein Treffen.
questions.answer: Entscheidend ist, dass viele Jugendliche zu wenig schlafen.
questions.explanation: 'Entscheidend ist, dass ...' announces the core claim. The other three sentences give single facts with no evaluation.

questions.prompt: The exam is modular. Which statement about your result is correct?
options: A weak part can be balanced out by a strong part.
Each part is passed or failed on its own, and only the failed part is retaken.
All four parts are added up into one single mark.
If one part is failed, the whole exam must be taken again.
questions.answer: Each part is passed or failed on its own, and only the failed part is retaken.
questions.explanation: Every module needs sixty percent by itself. There is no averaging, which is why you cannot afford to neglect your weakest skill.

questions.prompt: Complete the phrase that introduces a summary: 'Zusammenfassend kann man ____, dass sich der Kurs lohnt.'
questions.explanation: 'Zusammenfassend kann man sagen, dass ...' is the standard opening for a summarising statement, and the modal verb sends the infinitive to the end.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the verb for reading a text quickly to get the gist: 'Beim ersten Lesen ____ man den Text nur.'
questions.explanation: überfliegen means to skim. It is inseparable, so there is no 'fliegt über' here.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Read the questions before you read the text.'
questions.explanation: 'bevor' is a subordinating conjunction, so the verb 'liest' moves to the end of its clause.

activities.title: Wie liest du so schnell?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You explain to another candidate how you attack a long reading text and why you start with the questions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sag mal, wie schaffst du diese langen Texte so schnell?
lines.en: Tell me, how do you get through these long texts so quickly?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich lese zuerst die Fragen, dann weiß ich, wonach ich suchen muss.
lines.en: I read the questions first, then I know what I have to look for.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wenn du ein Wort nicht kennst?
lines.en: And if you do not know a word?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das überlese ich einfach, meistens brauche ich es für die Antwort gar nicht.
lines.en: I simply read over it, usually I do not need it for the answer at all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Liest du den ganzen Text von Anfang bis Ende?
lines.en: Do you read the whole text from beginning to end?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Beim ersten Mal überfliege ich ihn und achte auf den ersten und den letzten Satz.
lines.en: The first time I skim it and pay attention to the first and the last sentence.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Reicht dir die Zeit denn wirklich?
lines.en: Is the time really enough for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, denn ich verliere keine Minute mit Sätzen, die keine Frage beantworten.
lines.en: Yes, because I do not lose a minute on sentences that answer no question.

days.title: Lesen: Anzeigen zuordnen
days.objective: You can match short adverts to people's needs quickly by eliminating each advert on its disqualifying detail instead of hunting for the matching one.
days.paretoFocus: Matching tasks are pure speed: every advert you rule out in five seconds buys you a minute for the harder texts later in the reading part.
drills: Write down three conditions for an imaginary flat search, then invent four adverts and cross out three of them.
Take any list of small ads in German and mark the price, the time and the place in three different colours.
Practise saying in German why an offer does not fit, using 'weil' with the detail that rules it out.

items: Do you look for the matching detail or the disqualifying detail, and why?
Which three pieces of information usually decide a match in a small advert?
What do you answer when no advert fits the situation at all?

activities.title: Das Ausschlusskriterium finden
activities.intro: In the matching task you get short situations and a page of small adverts. Reading each advert fully is far too slow, so work by elimination: one wrong detail kills an advert, and you can move on. Remember that sometimes no advert fits, and that answer is deliberately included.

items.hr: Beim Zuordnen suchst du nicht die passende Anzeige, sondern du streichst die unpassenden weg.
items.en: When matching you do not look for the suitable advert, you cross out the unsuitable ones.
items.note: die Anzeige, die Anzeigen

items.hr: Ein einziges Detail kann eine Anzeige ausschließen, zum Beispiel der Preis, der Ort oder die Uhrzeit.
items.en: A single detail can rule out an advert, for example the price, the place or the time.
items.note: ausschließen, schließt aus, hat ausgeschlossen

items.hr: Unterstreiche in der Situation zuerst die drei Bedingungen: Wer, was und wann?
items.en: In the situation, first underline the three conditions: who, what and when?
items.note: die Bedingung, die Bedingungen

items.hr: Manchmal passt keine der Anzeigen, und dann ist genau das die richtige Antwort.
items.en: Sometimes none of the adverts fits, and then exactly that is the correct answer.
items.note: passen zu + Dativ

items.hr: Verliere keine Zeit mit einer Anzeige, die du schon einmal ausgeschlossen hast.
items.en: Do not lose time on an advert you have already ruled out.
items.note: der Zeitverlust, die Zeit

activities.title: Anzeigen prüfen und streichen
activities.intro: Practise finding the one detail that makes an offer impossible.

questions.prompt: Anna is looking for an evening language course costing no more than twenty euros an hour. Which detail rules an advert out?
options: Der Kurs findet dienstags um neunzehn Uhr statt.
Der Kurs kostet achtzehn Euro pro Stunde.
Der Kurs beginnt jeden Morgen um acht Uhr.
questions.answer: Der Kurs beginnt jeden Morgen um acht Uhr.
questions.explanation: A morning course cannot be an evening course, so this advert is out at once. Price and length still fit her conditions.

questions.prompt: What is the fastest way through a page of small adverts?
options: Read every advert completely and compare them at the end.
Translate each advert into your own language first.
Scan for the one detail that contradicts the situation and move on.
Choose the advert with the most information in it.
questions.answer: Scan for the one detail that contradicts the situation and move on.
questions.explanation: Elimination is much faster than confirmation, because one contradiction is enough while a match needs every condition checked.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the verb meaning to cross something out: 'Beim Zuordnen ____ du zuerst alles weg, was nicht passt.'
questions.explanation: wegstreichen is separable, so the prefix 'weg' stands at the end and the conjugated form takes second position.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the verb that means to rule out: 'Achte auf das Detail, das die Anzeige ____.'
questions.explanation: In a relative clause the verb goes to the end, and a separable verb is written as one word there.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'This offer does not fit because it is too expensive.'
questions.explanation: 'weil' sends the verb 'ist' to the very end of the second clause, after the adverbial 'zu teuer'.

activities.title: Welcher Kurs passt für Tom?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You help a friend match adverts to a person's needs by naming the detail that rules each one out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich soll für Tom einen Kurs finden, aber alle Anzeigen klingen gleich.
lines.en: I am supposed to find a course for Tom, but all the adverts sound the same.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Was braucht er denn genau? Schreib dir zuerst die Bedingungen auf.
lines.en: What exactly does he need? First write the conditions down.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Er kann nur am Wochenende, und mehr als hundert Euro will er nicht zahlen.
lines.en: He can only do weekends, and he does not want to pay more than a hundred euros.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dann streich alles weg, was unter der Woche stattfindet.
lines.en: Then cross out everything that takes place during the week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Diese Anzeige hier passt: samstags, neunzig Euro.
lines.en: This advert here fits: Saturdays, ninety euros.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vorsicht, da steht 'nur für Anfänger', und Tom lernt schon zwei Jahre.
lines.en: Careful, it says 'beginners only', and Tom has been learning for two years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Stimmt, dieses eine Wort schließt sie aus.
lines.en: True, that one word rules it out.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Genau, ein einziges Detail entscheidet, und danach musst du suchen.
lines.en: Exactly, a single detail decides it, and that is what you have to look for.

days.title: Lesen: Meinungen im Text erkennen
days.objective: You can tell an opinion from a fact in a German text by watching for signal verbs, the reported-speech Konjunktiv and evaluative adjectives.
days.paretoFocus: Half the questions in the harder reading texts ask what somebody thinks, not what happened, so the words that carry attitude are worth more than the content words.
drills: Take a German text and mark every verb of speaking, then decide whether it reports a fact or a judgement.
List five German adjectives that already contain an evaluation, and five that are purely descriptive.
Rewrite three factual sentences so that they become clearly marked opinions.

items: Which German verb tells you the writer doubts what is being reported?
What does the form 'sei' show you about who is speaking?
Give two adjectives that reveal a judgement rather than a fact.

activities.title: Meinung oder Tatsache?
activities.intro: German marks opinion openly through three signals. First, the reporting verb: behaupten, kritisieren, bezweifeln and betonen each carry a different attitude. Second, the Konjunktiv, which shows the words belong to somebody else, and third, adjectives that already contain a judgement.

items.hr: Der Autor behauptet, dass junge Leute heute weniger lesen als früher.
items.en: The author claims that young people read less today than they used to.
items.note: behaupten = to claim, without proof

items.hr: Die Journalistin kritisiert die neuen Regeln und hält sie für ungerecht.
items.en: The journalist criticises the new rules and considers them unfair.
items.note: halten für + Akkusativ

items.hr: Ein Fachmann bezweifelt, dass die Maßnahme wirklich hilft.
items.en: An expert doubts that the measure really helps.
items.note: die Maßnahme, die Maßnahmen

items.hr: Die Leiterin betont, dass die Eltern eine große Verantwortung tragen.
items.en: The director stresses that the parents carry a great responsibility.
items.note: die Verantwortung

items.hr: Er sagt, die Kritik sei übertrieben, und schon das Wort 'übertrieben' ist eine Bewertung.
items.en: He says the criticism is exaggerated, and the word 'übertrieben' alone is already a judgement.
items.note: sei = Konjunktiv I, marks reported words

activities.title: Signale für eine Bewertung
activities.intro: Practise separating what is reported as fact from what is presented as somebody's view.

questions.prompt: Which sentence states an opinion rather than a fact?
options: Die Stadt hat ein neues Schwimmbad gebaut.
Meiner Ansicht nach ist das Schwimmbad viel zu klein.
Das Schwimmbad öffnet um sieben Uhr.
questions.answer: Meiner Ansicht nach ist das Schwimmbad viel zu klein.
questions.explanation: 'Meiner Ansicht nach' plus 'viel zu klein' combines an opinion marker with an evaluative judgement. The others can be checked and are facts.

questions.prompt: Which verb shows that the writer does not believe the claim being reported?
questions.explanation: bezweifeln means to doubt. betonen stresses agreement, while berichten and beschreiben are neutral.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the reported-speech form that shows the writer is quoting somebody else: 'Der Sprecher sagt, die Lage ____ ernst.'
questions.explanation: Konjunktiv I 'sei' signals indirect speech: the writer passes the statement on without taking responsibility for it.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the verb that expresses disapproval: 'Die Autorin ____ die Pläne der Stadt und nennt sie unrealistisch.'
questions.explanation: kritisieren carries a negative evaluation, which is why the sentence is an opinion and not a report.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'He claims that nobody has read the report.'
questions.explanation: In a 'dass' clause the whole verb group moves to the end, so the participle comes before the auxiliary 'hat'.

activities.title: Was denkt der Autor wirklich?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You show a classmate how the verbs and adjectives in a text reveal the writer's position.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich verstehe den Text, aber ich weiß nie, was der Autor selbst denkt.
lines.en: I understand the text, but I never know what the author himself thinks.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Achte auf die Verben. Wenn dort 'behauptet' steht, glaubt er es meistens selbst nicht.
lines.en: Pay attention to the verbs. If it says 'behauptet', he usually does not believe it himself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und was bedeutet hier 'die Maßnahme sei sinnlos'?
lines.en: And what does 'die Maßnahme sei sinnlos' mean here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Diese Form zeigt, dass er nur wiedergibt, was jemand anderes gesagt hat.
lines.en: That form shows he is only passing on what somebody else said.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hier kritisiert eine Expertin die Pläne der Stadt.
lines.en: Here an expert criticises the city's plans.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dann hast du eine Meinung gefunden, denn Kritik ist nie eine reine Tatsache.
lines.en: Then you have found an opinion, because criticism is never a pure fact.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und die Adjektive?
lines.en: And the adjectives?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Wörter wie 'bedenklich' oder 'erfreulich' sind eine Bewertung, keine Information.
lines.en: Words like 'bedenklich' or 'erfreulich' are a judgement, not information.

days.title: Hören: das Wichtigste beim ersten Hören
days.objective: You can hold on to the speaker's intention during a single listening, instead of losing the thread while you puzzle over one unknown word.
days.paretoFocus: The listening part lasts about 40 minutes and some texts are played only once, so recovering fast from an unknown word is worth more than a bigger vocabulary.
drills: Listen to two minutes of German audio once and write the speaker's intention in one English sentence.
Play a short recording and count how many times you stop following it, then play it again and let the unknown words pass.
Before listening again, underline the key words in the questions and predict what you will hear.

items: What do you do the moment you meet a word you do not know?
How do you use the pause before a recording starts?
Name three small German words that reveal a speaker's attitude.

activities.title: Die Absicht der Sprecherin hören
activities.intro: The listening part is a module in its own right, passed or failed on its own at sixty percent, so a brilliant reading result cannot carry it. Some texts run only once, which means the real skill is staying with the speaker rather than understanding every word. Listen for intention and tone.

items.hr: Das Modul Hören dauert etwa vierzig Minuten, und manche Texte hörst du nur ein einziges Mal.
items.en: The listening module lasts about forty minutes, and some texts you hear only once.
items.note: das Modul Hören, einmal hören

items.hr: Achte nicht auf einzelne Wörter, sondern auf die Absicht der Sprecherin.
items.en: Do not pay attention to individual words, but to the speaker's intention.
items.note: die Absicht, die Absichten

items.hr: Wenn du ein Wort nicht verstehst, hör einfach weiter, sonst verpasst du den nächsten Satz.
items.en: If you do not understand a word, simply keep listening, otherwise you miss the next sentence.
items.note: verpassen, hat verpasst

items.hr: In der Pause vor dem Hörtext liest du die Aufgaben und unterstreichst die Schlüsselwörter.
items.en: In the pause before the recording you read the tasks and underline the key words.
items.note: das Schlüsselwort, die Schlüsselwörter

items.hr: Der Ton der Stimme verrät oft, ob jemand zufrieden, genervt oder unsicher ist.
items.en: The tone of the voice often reveals whether somebody is satisfied, annoyed or unsure.
items.note: die Stimme, verraten, hat verraten

activities.title: Haltung und Ton verstehen
activities.intro: Practise reading attitude out of a spoken sentence and handling the words you miss.

questions.prompt: You hear: 'Na ja, der Film war schon in Ordnung, aber ich hatte mir mehr erwartet.' What is the speaker's attitude?
questions.explanation: 'Na ja' plus 'schon in Ordnung' plus 'aber' softens the praise and then withdraws it, which is disappointment rather than anger.

questions.prompt: A word in the recording is completely unknown to you. What is the right reaction?
options: Stop and try to work the word out from its parts.
Keep listening and rely on the rest of the sentence.
Write the word down phonetically and think about it.
Skip the whole question and wait for the next text.
questions.answer: Keep listening and rely on the rest of the sentence.
questions.explanation: The recording does not wait. One unknown word costs you nothing, but the sentences you miss while thinking about it usually cost you the answer.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap: 'Wenn du ein Wort nicht kennst, ____ du trotzdem weiter.'
questions.explanation: After the subordinate clause with 'wenn', the main clause starts with the conjugated verb, so 'hörst' comes before the subject.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the verb meaning to underline: 'In der Pause vor dem Text ____ du die Schlüsselwörter.'
questions.explanation: unterstreichen is inseparable here, so the prefix stays attached and the ending agrees with 'du'.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I did not understand every word, but the meaning was clear.'
questions.explanation: 'aber' is a coordinating conjunction, so the second clause keeps normal word order with the verb in second position.

activities.title: Ich verliere immer den Faden
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You advise a candidate who freezes on unknown words during listening tasks.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Beim Hören verliere ich nach zwei Sätzen den Faden.
lines.en: When listening I lose the thread after two sentences.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das passiert, wenn du an einem Wort hängen bleibst. Lass es einfach los.
lines.en: That happens when you get stuck on one word. Just let it go.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Aber dann verstehe ich die Aufgabe doch nicht.
lines.en: But then I do not understand the task after all.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Du brauchst selten das einzelne Wort, du brauchst die Absicht der Sprecherin.
lines.en: You rarely need the individual word, you need the speaker's intention.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wie erkenne ich die?
lines.en: And how do I recognise it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Am Ton und an Wörtern wie 'leider', 'endlich' oder 'eigentlich'.
lines.en: From the tone and from words like 'leider', 'endlich' or 'eigentlich'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Was machst du in der Pause vor dem Text?
lines.en: What do you do in the pause before the text?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich lese die Aufgaben und markiere die Wörter, auf die ich hören will.
lines.en: I read the tasks and mark the words I want to listen out for.

days.title: Hören: Ansagen und Durchsagen
days.objective: You can pull the decisive information out of a public announcement by listening for the change to what was expected, not for the whole message.
days.paretoFocus: Announcements are built around one correction, a platform, a time or a cancellation, and the same handful of phrases carries it every time.
drills: Write three announcements in German in which a time, a place and a service each change.
Say the numbers from one to twenty out loud in German twice, quickly, until they cost you no thinking time.
Listen to any German announcement and note only what is different from normal.

items: Which German phrase tells you a service has been cancelled completely?
Which word usually comes right before the corrected information?
How do you say that an arrival is running fifteen minutes late?

activities.title: Durchsagen am Bahnhof und im Geschäft
activities.intro: Public announcements follow a fixed shape: polite opening, the thing affected, then the change. The answer is almost always in the change, so listen for what is different from the timetable rather than for the whole sentence. The words after 'nicht' and 'statt' are where the information hides.

items.hr: Der Zug nach Hamburg fällt heute leider aus.
items.en: The train to Hamburg is unfortunately cancelled today.
items.note: der Zug, ausfallen, fällt aus, ist ausgefallen

items.hr: Die Ankunft verzögert sich um etwa zwanzig Minuten.
items.en: The arrival is delayed by about twenty minutes.
items.note: die Ankunft, sich verzögern

items.hr: Der Zug fährt heute nicht von Gleis drei, sondern von Gleis neun ab.
items.en: Today the train departs not from platform three but from platform nine.
items.note: das Gleis, die Gleise

items.hr: Liebe Kundinnen und Kunden, unser Markt schließt in zehn Minuten.
items.en: Dear customers, our shop closes in ten minutes.
items.note: der Kunde, die Kundin

items.hr: Bitte beachten Sie: Der Aufzug ist wegen einer Reparatur außer Betrieb.
items.en: Please note: the lift is out of service because of a repair.
items.note: der Aufzug, die Reparatur, der Betrieb

activities.title: Die Änderung heraushören
activities.intro: Practise catching the corrected detail in a short public announcement.

questions.prompt: You hear: 'Der Zug nach Köln fährt heute nicht von Gleis vier, sondern von Gleis elf ab.' Where do you go?
questions.explanation: The information after 'sondern' replaces the information after 'nicht'. The first number you hear is the one that no longer applies.

questions.prompt: Which expression tells you a train will not run at all today?
questions.explanation: ausfallen means to be cancelled. 'verzögert sich' is only a delay, 'fährt durch' means it does not stop, and 'hält zusätzlich' adds a stop.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap: 'Der Aufzug ist wegen einer Reparatur außer ____.'
questions.explanation: 'außer Betrieb' is the fixed phrase for out of service, and the noun keeps its capital letter without an article in this expression.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the verb for running late: 'Die Abfahrt ____ sich um fünfzehn Minuten.'
questions.explanation: Both sich verzögern and sich verspäten are used for delays, and the reflexive pronoun 'sich' stays with the verb.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Please note that the shop closes earlier today.'
questions.explanation: The polite imperative puts 'Sie' after the verb, and the 'dass' clause sends 'schließt' to the end.

activities.title: Eine Durchsage am Bahnsteig
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You catch the changed details in a station announcement and explain them to a traveller who only heard the numbers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Achtung, eine Durchsage. Hast du etwas verstanden?
lines.en: Attention, an announcement. Did you understand anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, unser Zug fährt heute von Gleis elf ab, nicht von Gleis vier.
lines.en: Yes, our train departs from platform eleven today, not from platform four.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und warum stehen hier so viele Leute?
lines.en: And why are so many people standing here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Weil der Zug davor ausfällt, das haben sie ganz am Anfang gesagt.
lines.en: Because the train before ours is cancelled, they said that right at the beginning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kommen wir denn trotzdem pünktlich an?
lines.en: Will we still arrive on time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Die Ankunft verzögert sich um etwa zehn Minuten, mehr nicht.
lines.en: The arrival is delayed by about ten minutes, no more.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich habe wirklich nur die Zahlen gehört.
lines.en: I really only heard the numbers.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Die Zahlen helfen, aber wichtig ist das Wort nach 'nicht', dort steht die Änderung.
lines.en: The numbers help, but the important thing is the word after 'nicht', that is where the change is.

days.title: Schreiben: ein Forumsbeitrag mit eigener Meinung
days.objective: You can write an informal forum post with a clear four-part structure that covers every bullet point in the task and states a reasoned opinion.
days.paretoFocus: The marks in the writing part are attached to the bullet points you were given, so a well structured post that answers all of them beats a beautiful post that answers three.
drills: Take any opinion question and plan a forum post in two minutes: opening, opinion, reason, example, closing question.
Write four bullet points for yourself and then write one German sentence for each, in order.
Rewrite a formal sentence you know into relaxed forum language addressing the group as 'ihr'.

items: How long does the writing part last, and how is it assessed against the other parts?
Name the four steps of a forum post from opening to closing.
Which greeting and which sign-off suit a forum but not a formal letter?

activities.title: Ein Forumsbeitrag in vier Schritten
activities.intro: The writing part lasts about sixty minutes and is a module of its own, so it must reach sixty percent by itself. Spend the first two minutes planning: a forum post opens with a greeting, states your opinion with a reason, adds your own experience, and closes with a question to the others. Every bullet point in the task must appear, because that is where the marks sit.

items.hr: Hallo zusammen, ich habe euren Beitrag mit großem Interesse gelesen.
items.en: Hello everyone, I read your post with great interest.
items.note: der Beitrag, die Beiträge

items.hr: Meiner Meinung nach sollte man Handys in der Schule nur in den Pausen erlauben.
items.en: In my opinion, mobile phones in school should only be allowed during the breaks.
items.note: die Meinung, die Pause

items.hr: Ich sehe das so, weil viele Schüler sonst im Unterricht abgelenkt sind.
items.en: I see it that way because otherwise many pupils are distracted in class.
items.note: der Unterricht, abgelenkt sein

items.hr: Bei uns zu Hause war das früher anders, und das hat gut funktioniert.
items.en: At home with us it used to be different, and that worked well.
items.note: funktionieren, hat funktioniert

items.hr: Was denkt ihr darüber? Viele Grüße, Lena
items.en: What do you think about it? Best wishes, Lena
items.note: informal sign-off, 'ihr' for the group

activities.title: Aufbau und Ton eines Beitrags
activities.intro: Practise the register and the structure that a forum post needs.

questions.prompt: Which opening suits a forum post rather than a formal letter?
questions.explanation: A forum addresses a group informally with 'ihr'. The 'Sehr geehrte' forms belong to a formal letter, and 'Mit freundlichen Grüßen' is a closing, not an opening.

questions.prompt: The task gives you four points and you cover only three. Why does that cost you badly?
options: The text becomes too short to read.
The marks are attached to the given points, so a missing point is a lost mark.
The grammar is judged as incorrect.
Only the first point is ever assessed.
questions.answer: The marks are attached to the given points, so a missing point is a lost mark.
questions.explanation: Content is graded against the bullet points. Elegant language cannot replace a point you never mentioned, so tick them off as you write.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap so the phrase means 'in my opinion': '____ Meinung nach ist ein Verbot übertrieben.'
questions.explanation: The fixed phrase takes the dative, and 'die Meinung' is feminine, so the possessive ends in 'er'.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap in the sign-off of an informal post: 'Was denkt ihr darüber? Viele ____, Jonas'
questions.explanation: 'Viele Grüße' is the standard friendly closing, and the noun is written with ß after the long vowel.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I think that everybody should decide for themselves.'
questions.explanation: In the 'dass' clause the modal verb 'sollte' takes the final position, after the infinitive.

activities.title: Wie fange ich den Beitrag an?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You talk a friend through the structure of a forum post and remind them about the bullet points.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich muss einen Beitrag ins Forum schreiben und weiß nicht, wie ich anfange.
lines.en: I have to write a post in the forum and do not know how to start.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fang mit 'Hallo zusammen' an und sag in einem Satz, worum es geht.
lines.en: Start with 'Hallo zusammen' and say in one sentence what it is about.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und dann schreibe ich direkt meine Meinung?
lines.en: And then I write my opinion straight away?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, aber immer mit einer Begründung, sonst bleibt der Text leer.
lines.en: Yes, but always with a reason, otherwise the text stays empty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: In der Aufgabe stehen vier Punkte.
lines.en: There are four points in the task.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dann müssen alle vier vorkommen, denn genau darauf gibt es die Punkte.
lines.en: Then all four have to appear, because that is exactly what the marks are for.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wie beende ich den Beitrag am besten?
lines.en: How do I best end the post?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mit einer Frage an die anderen und einem kurzen Gruß, das wirkt natürlich.
lines.en: With a question to the others and a short greeting, that sounds natural.

days.title: Schreiben: eine formelle E-Mail
days.objective: You can write a formal email in German with the correct greeting, a clear reason for writing, a polite request and the standard closing formula.
days.paretoFocus: The formal register is a small fixed set of phrases, so once the frame is automatic you only have to invent the content in the middle.
drills: Write out the formal frame from greeting to sign-off from memory, with nothing in the middle.
Turn three informal requests into polite questions with 'Könnten Sie mir bitte ...'.
Check a text you have written and make sure every 'Sie', 'Ihnen' and 'Ihr' is capitalised.

items: Which greeting do you use when you do not know the person's name, and which closing goes with it?
How do you say in German that you are writing because of a course?
Give one polite way to ask for information without using an imperative.

activities.title: Die formelle E-Mail von der Anrede bis zum Gruß
activities.intro: A formal email has a fixed skeleton: greeting, reason for writing, the body covering every required point, a request, and the closing formula. Use 'Sie' throughout and capitalise it along with 'Ihnen' and 'Ihr'. Planning the body around the given points before you write costs two minutes and saves the whole text.

items.hr: Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, ich schreibe Ihnen wegen des Kurses im September.
items.en: Dear Sir or Madam, I am writing to you about the course in September.
items.note: der Kurs, wegen + Genitiv

items.hr: Leider habe ich auf meine Anfrage vom letzten Monat noch keine Antwort bekommen.
items.en: Unfortunately I have not yet received a reply to my enquiry from last month.
items.note: die Anfrage, die Antwort

items.hr: Könnten Sie mir bitte mitteilen, ob noch Plätze frei sind?
items.en: Could you please let me know whether there are still places available?
items.note: der Platz, die Plätze, mitteilen

items.hr: Über eine kurze Rückmeldung würde ich mich sehr freuen.
items.en: I would be very glad to receive a short reply.
items.note: die Rückmeldung, sich freuen über + Akkusativ

items.hr: Vielen Dank im Voraus. Mit freundlichen Grüßen, Martin Keller
items.en: Many thanks in advance. Kind regards, Martin Keller
items.note: der Dank, im Voraus

activities.title: Höflich und korrekt schreiben
activities.intro: Practise the fixed phrases and the polite forms that hold a formal email together.

questions.prompt: You do not know the name of the person who will read your email. Which pair of greeting and closing is correct?
options: Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, ... Mit freundlichen Grüßen
questions.answer: Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, ... Mit freundlichen Grüßen
questions.explanation: The unnamed formal greeting always pairs with the neutral formal closing. Mixing an informal greeting with a formal closing breaks the register.

questions.prompt: Which sentence keeps the formal register correctly?
options: Kannst du mir bitte sagen, wann der Kurs anfängt?
Sag mir bitte, ob noch etwas frei ist.
Ich hätte gern gewusst, ob Sie mir helfen können.
questions.answer: Ich hätte gern gewusst, ob Sie mir helfen können.
questions.explanation: Only this option uses 'Sie' and a softened Konjunktiv form. The other three use 'du' or a bare imperative, which is too direct for a formal email.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap in the greeting to an unknown recipient: 'Sehr ____ Damen und Herren,'
questions.explanation: The participle takes the plural adjective ending after 'sehr', and the phrase is always written exactly this way.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap in the standard formal sign-off: 'Mit ____ Grüßen'
questions.explanation: 'mit' takes the dative, so the plural adjective ending is 'en'. Note the ß in 'Grüßen' after the long vowel.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Could you please tell me when the course begins?'
questions.explanation: The polite question starts with the Konjunktiv form 'Könnten', and the indirect question with 'wann' sends 'beginnt' to the end.

activities.title: Hilfe bei einer formellen E-Mail
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You dictate the formal frame to a friend who wants to write to a company.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich schreibe an eine Firma. Kann ich einfach 'Hallo' schreiben?
lines.en: I am writing to a company. Can I simply write 'Hallo'?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nein, wenn du den Namen nicht kennst, schreibst du 'Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren'.
lines.en: No, if you do not know the name, you write 'Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wie sage ich, warum ich überhaupt schreibe?
lines.en: And how do I say why I am writing at all?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gleich im ersten Satz: 'Ich schreibe Ihnen, weil ich eine Frage zum Kurs habe.'
lines.en: Right in the first sentence: 'I am writing to you because I have a question about the course.'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Muss ich 'Sie' wirklich großschreiben?
lines.en: Do I really have to capitalise 'Sie'?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, 'Sie', 'Ihnen' und 'Ihr' schreibt man in der höflichen Anrede immer groß.
lines.en: Yes, 'Sie', 'Ihnen' and 'Ihr' are always capitalised in the polite form of address.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wie höre ich am Ende auf?
lines.en: And how do I finish at the end?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mit 'Über eine kurze Rückmeldung würde ich mich freuen' und dann 'Mit freundlichen Grüßen'.
lines.en: With 'I would be glad to receive a short reply' and then 'Kind regards'.

days.title: Schreiben: eine Beschwerde
days.objective: You can write a calm, factual complaint in German that states what happened, what the problem is, what you want and by when.
days.paretoFocus: A complaint is judged on clarity, not on anger, and the same four moves work for a delivery, a repair, a booking or a bill.
drills: Write the four moves of a complaint in English, then write one German sentence for each.
Take an angry sentence and rewrite it factually, keeping the same demand.
Practise three ways of setting a deadline in German using 'bis zum' with a date.

items: In which order do the four parts of a complaint come?
How do you politely state what you expect the other side to do?
Which auxiliary does 'stehen' take in the perfect in standard German?

activities.title: Sachlich sich beschweren
activities.intro: A complaint works in four moves: the facts with a date, the problem, the demand, and the deadline. Stay factual, because a calm text is far more convincing than an angry one, and keep the formal frame you already know. Note that stehen, sitzen and liegen build the perfect with haben in standard German.

items.hr: Am dritten Mai habe ich bei Ihnen einen Schreibtisch bestellt.
items.en: On the third of May I ordered a desk from you.
items.note: der Schreibtisch, bestellen, hat bestellt

items.hr: Leider war die Lieferung beschädigt, und ein Bein fehlt völlig.
items.en: Unfortunately the delivery was damaged, and one leg is missing entirely.
items.note: die Lieferung, das Bein

items.hr: Das Paket hat drei Tage im Flur gestanden, bevor ich es öffnen konnte.
items.en: The parcel stood in the hallway for three days before I could open it.
items.note: stehen builds the perfect with haben: hat gestanden

items.hr: Ich bitte Sie deshalb, mir bis zum zwanzigsten Mai einen Ersatz zu schicken.
items.en: I therefore ask you to send me a replacement by the twentieth of May.
items.note: der Ersatz, bitten um + Akkusativ

items.hr: Sollte ich bis dahin nichts von Ihnen hören, trete ich vom Vertrag zurück.
items.en: Should I not hear from you by then, I will withdraw from the contract.
items.note: der Vertrag, zurücktreten, tritt zurück

activities.title: Eine Beschwerde formulieren
activities.intro: Practise the wording of a factual complaint and the perfect forms that go with it.

questions.prompt: Which perfect form is correct in the standard German of Germany?
options: Das Paket ist im Flur gestanden.
Das Paket hat im Flur gestanden.
Das Paket ist im Flur gestehen.
Das Paket hat im Flur gestellt.
questions.answer: Das Paket hat im Flur gestanden.
questions.explanation: stehen takes haben in standard German, so 'hat gestanden'. The form with 'ist' is regional and counts as wrong here, and 'gestellt' belongs to a different verb.

questions.prompt: Which sentence states a demand in the right tone for a written complaint?
options: Das ist eine Frechheit, ich will sofort mein Geld!
Ich bitte Sie, mir den Betrag bis Freitag zurückzuzahlen.
Vielleicht könnten Sie irgendwann etwas machen.
questions.answer: Ich bitte Sie, mir den Betrag bis Freitag zurückzuzahlen.
questions.explanation: It is polite, names exactly what is wanted and sets a date. The others are either aggressive, too vague to act on, or in the wrong register.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the verb for making a polite request: 'Ich ____ Sie, mir bis Freitag zu antworten.'
questions.explanation: bitten plus an accusative person and a 'zu' infinitive is the standard polite request pattern in a formal letter.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the adjective meaning damaged: 'Leider war die Ware bei der Ankunft ____.'
questions.explanation: As a predicate adjective after 'sein' the participle takes no ending, so it stays 'beschädigt'.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Unfortunately I have not received an answer so far.'
questions.explanation: Starting with 'Leider' pushes the subject behind the verb, and the participle 'bekommen' closes the sentence.

activities.title: Die Lampe kam kaputt an
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You coach a friend on how to complain in writing about a damaged delivery.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Die Lampe ist kaputt angekommen. Soll ich einfach dort anrufen?
lines.en: The lamp arrived broken. Should I just call them?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Schreib lieber, dann hast du alles schriftlich. Beginne mit dem Datum der Bestellung.
lines.en: Better write, then you have everything in writing. Start with the date of the order.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und danach beschreibe ich den Schaden?
lines.en: And after that I describe the damage?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Genau, sachlich und kurz, ohne Wut, denn das wirkt viel stärker.
lines.en: Exactly, factually and briefly, without anger, because that has a much stronger effect.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Was fordere ich denn konkret?
lines.en: What exactly do I demand then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sag klar, was du willst, und nenne eine Frist, zum Beispiel bis zum Ende der Woche.
lines.en: Say clearly what you want and name a deadline, for example by the end of the week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wenn dann immer noch nichts passiert?
lines.en: And if still nothing happens then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dann kündigst du höflich an, dass du vom Vertrag zurücktrittst.
lines.en: Then you politely announce that you will withdraw from the contract.

days.title: Sprechen: eine kurze Präsentation halten
days.objective: You can give a short structured presentation that introduces the topic, gives your own experience, describes the situation in your home country, weighs advantages and disadvantages and ends with an opinion.
days.paretoFocus: A memorised structure means you never have to invent the shape of the talk, only the content, which is what keeps you fluent under pressure.
drills: Choose a everyday topic and speak for two minutes following the five steps in order.
Prepare one sentence of personal experience and one about your home country for three different topics.
Practise naming one advantage and one disadvantage of the same thing in consecutive sentences.

items: What are the five parts of the short presentation, in order?
How do you introduce the topic of your talk in one German sentence?
Which phrase do you use to close with your own view?

activities.title: Fünf Schritte für eine Präsentation
activities.intro: The speaking part lasts about fifteen minutes and is taken in pairs, but it is still a module of its own that must reach sixty percent by itself. Your short talk follows five steps: topic, your own experience, the situation in your home country, advantages and disadvantages, and your conclusion. Learn the linking sentences and you only have to think about content.

items.hr: In meiner Präsentation geht es um das Thema Einkaufen im Internet.
items.en: My presentation is about the topic of shopping on the internet.
items.note: die Präsentation, das Thema

items.hr: Ich selbst bestelle etwa einmal im Monat etwas online, meistens Bücher.
items.en: I myself order something online about once a month, mostly books.
items.note: bestellen, das Buch, die Bücher

items.hr: In meinem Heimatland ist das anders, dort gehen die meisten Leute in kleine Geschäfte.
items.en: In my home country it is different, there most people go to small shops.
items.note: das Heimatland, das Geschäft

items.hr: Ein Vorteil ist die große Auswahl, ein Nachteil ist die lange Wartezeit.
items.en: One advantage is the wide choice, one disadvantage is the long waiting time.
items.note: der Vorteil, der Nachteil, die Auswahl

items.hr: Insgesamt überwiegen für mich die Vorteile, und deshalb bestelle ich weiter online.
items.en: All in all the advantages outweigh for me, and that is why I go on ordering online.
items.note: closing move: insgesamt plus your view

activities.title: Den Vortrag aufbauen
activities.intro: Practise the order of the parts and the sentences that join them.

questions.prompt: Which order do the parts of the short talk follow?
options: Meinung, Thema, Erfahrung, Heimatland, Vor- und Nachteile
Thema, Erfahrung, Heimatland, Vor- und Nachteile, Meinung
Vor- und Nachteile, Meinung, Thema, Erfahrung, Heimatland
Heimatland, Meinung, Thema, Vor- und Nachteile, Erfahrung
questions.answer: Thema, Erfahrung, Heimatland, Vor- und Nachteile, Meinung
questions.explanation: You open by naming the topic and close with your own view, so the opinion belongs at the end, after the arguments that support it.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is a proper conclusion for the talk?
options: Insgesamt finde ich, dass die Vorteile größer sind.
Heute spreche ich über das Wetter.
In meinem Heimatland ist es warm.
questions.answer: Insgesamt finde ich, dass die Vorteile größer sind.
questions.explanation: 'Insgesamt' signals the summing up and is followed by a stated position, which is exactly what the closing move needs.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the noun meaning advantage: 'Ein ____ ist die große Auswahl im Internet.'
questions.explanation: der Vorteil is masculine, so the indefinite article is 'ein', and the German noun is capitalised.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap so the sentence announces your subject: 'In meiner Präsentation ____ es um das Thema Sport.'
questions.explanation: 'Es geht um' plus accusative is the fixed way of saying what something is about, and the verb keeps second position after the fronted phrase.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'In my home country most people cook at home.'
questions.explanation: The fronted place phrase takes position one, so the verb 'kochen' stands second and the subject follows it.

activities.title: Meine Präsentation über das Fahrrad
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You answer a listener's questions using the five steps of the presentation structure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Worüber sprichst du in deiner Präsentation?
lines.en: What are you speaking about in your presentation?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: In meiner Präsentation geht es um das Thema Fahrradfahren in der Stadt.
lines.en: My presentation is about the topic of cycling in the city.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hast du selbst Erfahrung damit?
lines.en: Do you have experience with it yourself?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, ich fahre jeden Tag zur Arbeit, auch im Winter.
lines.en: Yes, I ride to work every day, even in winter.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wie ist die Situation in deinem Heimatland?
lines.en: And what is the situation like in your home country?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dort fahren nur wenige Leute mit dem Rad, weil es kaum sichere Wege gibt.
lines.en: There only few people cycle, because there are hardly any safe paths.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gibt es denn auch Nachteile?
lines.en: Are there disadvantages as well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ein Nachteil ist das Wetter, aber insgesamt überwiegen für mich die Vorteile.
lines.en: One disadvantage is the weather, but all in all the advantages outweigh for me.

days.title: Sprechen: gemeinsam etwas planen
days.objective: You can plan something together with a partner by making a suggestion, asking what they think, reacting to their answer and reaching a joint decision.
days.paretoFocus: Candidates lose this task by talking without stopping, so the single most valuable habit is ending every suggestion with a question to your partner.
drills: Make five suggestions out loud in German and add a different question to your partner after each one.
Practise three ways of half agreeing before you propose an alternative.
Plan an imaginary outing in four turns and finish with a sentence that fixes the decision.

items: What must follow every suggestion you make in the planning task?
Give two German ways of asking your partner for their opinion.
Which sentence shows that you and your partner have agreed?

activities.title: Vorschlagen, fragen, reagieren, entscheiden
activities.intro: In the paired planning task you are assessed on interaction, not on a solo speech. The cycle is always the same: make a suggestion, ask what your partner thinks, react to the answer, and then fix the decision together. A candidate who only lists ideas loses marks even with perfect grammar.

items.hr: Ich schlage vor, dass wir das Fest am Samstagnachmittag machen.
items.en: I suggest that we hold the party on Saturday afternoon.
items.note: das Fest, vorschlagen, schlägt vor

items.hr: Wie wäre es, wenn wir den Kuchen selbst backen? Was hältst du davon?
items.en: How about baking the cake ourselves? What do you think of that?
items.note: der Kuchen, halten von + Dativ

items.hr: Das ist eine gute Idee, aber ich hätte lieber etwas mehr Zeit.
items.en: That is a good idea, but I would prefer a bit more time.
items.note: die Idee, hätte lieber = Konjunktiv II

items.hr: Da bin ich ganz deiner Meinung, dann machen wir es so.
items.en: I completely agree with you there, so let us do it that way.
items.note: fixes the joint decision

items.hr: Wer nur redet und den Partner nie fragt, verliert Punkte in diesem Teil.
items.en: Anyone who only talks and never asks their partner loses marks in this part.
items.note: der Partner, der Punkt, die Punkte

activities.title: Gemeinsam zu einer Lösung kommen
activities.intro: Practise the turn-taking phrases that keep a planning conversation alive.

questions.prompt: Which reply invites your partner into the planning instead of closing the topic?
options: Wir treffen uns um zehn Uhr, fertig.
Ich möchte lieber ins Kino, das steht fest.
Ich würde gern ins Museum gehen. Wäre dir das recht?
questions.answer: Ich würde gern ins Museum gehen. Wäre dir das recht?
questions.explanation: The suggestion is followed by a direct question, which hands the turn over. The other options state a decision and leave the partner nothing to answer.

questions.prompt: Why does a candidate who presents all the ideas alone still lose marks here?
options: The task is assessed on interaction, so questions and reactions are part of the mark.
Speaking a lot is always penalised in German.
Only the partner is assessed in this task.
The ideas have to be written down first.
questions.answer: The task is assessed on interaction, so questions and reactions are part of the mark.
questions.explanation: This part measures whether you can negotiate. Reacting to what your partner says and agreeing on a plan is the skill being tested.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap so you ask your partner for an opinion: 'Was ____ du davon, wenn wir uns um vier treffen?'
questions.explanation: halten von is the standard way of asking for someone's view, and the 'du' form has a vowel change to 'hältst'.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap to confirm a joint decision: 'Einverstanden, dann ____ wir das so.'
questions.explanation: After the fronted 'dann' the verb keeps second position, so it comes before the subject 'wir'.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'How about meeting at the station?'
questions.explanation: 'Wie wäre es, wenn ...' uses Konjunktiv II for a soft suggestion, and 'wenn' sends 'treffen' to the end.

activities.title: Wir planen einen Ausflug
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Every time you make a suggestion, ask your partner what they think, then react and settle the plan together.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wir sollen zusammen einen Ausflug für die Gruppe planen. Hast du eine Idee?
lines.en: We are supposed to plan an outing for the group together. Do you have an idea?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich schlage vor, dass wir an den See fahren. Was hältst du davon?
lines.en: I suggest that we go to the lake. What do you think of that?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Der See ist schön, aber die Fahrt dauert fast zwei Stunden.
lines.en: The lake is lovely, but the journey takes almost two hours.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Stimmt. Wie wäre es dann mit dem Park am Fluss? Wäre dir das lieber?
lines.en: True. How about the park by the river then? Would you prefer that?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja, das gefällt mir besser. Und wann treffen wir uns?
lines.en: Yes, I like that better. And when shall we meet?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Um zehn Uhr am Eingang, oder ist dir das zu früh?
lines.en: At ten o'clock at the entrance, or is that too early for you?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zehn Uhr passt mir gut.
lines.en: Ten o'clock suits me well.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut, dann machen wir es so: Du bringst die Getränke und ich das Essen.
lines.en: Good, then we will do it like this: you bring the drinks and I bring the food.
